Name: Climate Change Writers
Description:
Climate Change Writers is a sub-entity of the TeraShift Sustainability Initiative.

Climate Change Writers wants to reach the world. We are writers from all over the planet and our goal is to create awareness about the impact climate change is having on our lives; in ways that leave no details out.

We believe that only by doing this can we set things straight and prepare to utilize our planet's resources in ways that are sustainable and efficient.

Name: MCS
Description:
With energy costs constantly rising and climate change affecting us all – low-carbon technology has a bigger and bigger role to play in the future of UK energy.

We’re here to ensure it’s a positive one.

Working with industry we define, maintain and improve quality – certifying products and installers so people can have confidence in the low-carbon technology they invest in. From solar and wind, to heat pumps, biomass and battery storage, we want to inspire a new generation of home-grown energy, fit for the needs of every UK home and community.

Name: PIF 2 | Projet d'Investissement Forestier Côte d’Ivoire - phase 2
Description:
Le Projet d’Investissement Forestier - phase 2 (PIF 2) est un projet du Gouvernement Ivoirien conduit par le Ministère des Eaux et Forêts et financé par la Banque mondiale. Entré en vigueur en 2022 pour une durée de 7 ans, il a pour objectif de conserver et augmenter le stock forestier, et d'améliorer l'accès aux sources de revenus issus de la gestion durable des forêts dans ses zones d’intervention. 

Pour ce faire, le PIF 2 est structuré en 5 composantes pour apporter un appui à : l’élaboration des Plans d’Aménagement Participatifs (PAPF) des Forêts Classées (FC) (1), la mise en œuvre des Plans d’Aménagement Participatifs des FC C3 (2) ; la gestion durable des Parcs Nationaux et Réserves Naturelles (3) ; la mise en œuvre des Plans d’Aménagement Participatifs des FC C4 de la zone de savane (4). Le projet assure également l’administration, la coordination et les aspects de sauvegardes (5).

Name: International Student Environmental Coalition-Chad
Description:
International Student Environmental Coalition - Chad is a chapter of a global movement of young environmental advocates working to advance the skills, strengths and power of students and students to forcefully move the world in a more just, more inclusive direction and more durable.

Our mission is to give young leaders the means, the tools and the key to fight against climate change and environmental challenges as a conscious and acting generation.

Our vision is to build a bridge between the actions our community needs today and a green future free from climate change, desertification, plastic waste and fossil fuels where young people can dream bigger and invest fully in achieving the United Nations SDGs by 2030 and updating the African Union's 2063 agenda.

Young people have the power to solve the challenges we face in our community. They are not only the actors of the future, but also the leaders of today, yet they are ignored. We work to activate their leadership so that they can lead the resolution of these challenges to defend their hopes.

Our Approaches

Education
We make environmental education for all, our workhorse and the early awareness of future generations, our aim to put young people in the capacity to understand the issues of environmental protection from the ground up.

Leadership
We identify junior leaders, we elevate them and amplify their voice, equipping them with the right tools and mindset to awaken their genius for creativity and green influence helping their schools and communities solve the most environmental challenges.

Innovation
We continually strive to seek to develop new solutions, visions and methods to better solve the problems of our community, for this we think creatively and innovatively.

Impact
We aim for meaningful impact on a personal and collective level that can be measurable and tangible that can be seen, felt and touched at all levels of our community and beyond.

Name: Go Forest
Description:
Forests slow down climate change and are the most important reservoir of biodiversity. Unfortunately, deforestation weighs heavily on our planet. That’s why Go Forest offers accessible and transparent solutions to plant trees with impact. Let’s plan(t) a greener future together. Not only for us, but also for our future generations. 

www.goforest.be

Name: Green Earth Resilience Foundation
Description:
Green Earth Resilience Foundation is a forward-thinking organization committed to a sustainable world. Led by passionate youth leaders, we champion the fight against climate change, environmental degradation, and deforestation while raising public awareness. Our mission extends to actively supporting the United Nations Development Programme's Sustainable Development Goal 13 (SDG-13).

Central to our mission is engaging the younger generation. We recognize that the future of our planet lies in their hands, and we are dedicated to educating, empowering, and inspiring school and college students. Our goal is to nurture responsibility and environmental consciousness among the youth to ensure a brighter and more sustainable tomorrow.

Our initiatives encompass a wide range of activities, from impactful tree-planting campaigns aiding afforestation efforts to educational programs addressing the complex challenges posed by climate change. We firmly believe that knowledge is the catalyst for transformation, and by equipping young people with the tools to understand and combat these challenges, we are building a formidable force for change.

At the core of Green Earth Resilience Foundation's vision is our motto, "Together for a better tomorrow." It reflects our belief that by uniting individuals and communities in the battle against environmental crises, we can collectively make a substantial impact. Our dedication, fueled by a sense of urgency and profound love for the planet, inspires hope and instills a sense of responsibility in all those who encounter our work.

In a world where the consequences of climate change are increasingly evident, Green Earth Resilience Foundation stands as a guiding light. Our unwavering commitment to forging a sustainable future, coupled with our unwavering focus on empowering youth, transcends being merely an organization – we are a transformative movement dedicated to ushering in positive change for our beloved planet. Together with the next

The Mission of ECO Platform is to promote and to contribute to the sustainable development, including a low-carbon economy and resource efficiency in the construction sector, by coordinating the development and provision of credible and scientifically correct data from products.

Name: HCS-Hemp Carbon Standard
Description:
At Hemp Carbon Standard (HCS), we harness the transformative potential of industrial hemp to revolutionize agriculture and combat climate change. Our mission is to build a sustainable financial ecosystem that empowers hemp farmers while driving measurable environmental impact.

Through verified data and cutting-edge technology, we enable hemp growers to monetize their contributions to carbon removal and reduction. By creating high-integrity carbon credits, we connect farmers with global carbon markets, fostering trust, transparency, and scalability.

HCS is more than a carbon platform—we’re a catalyst for change, supporting agricultural innovation, economic growth, and a healthier planet for future generations.

Name: Association Biocontrôle et Biostimulation pour l'Agroécologie
Description:
L'association vise à porter le Grand Défi biocontrôle et biostimulation pour l'Agroécologie.
Le Grand Défi est piloté par le ministère de l’Agriculture et de la Souveraineté Alimentaire. L’Etat français prévoit de soutenir ces travaux grâce à l’apport de 42 millions d’euros gérés par l’Agence Nationale de la Recherche et 18 millions d’euros apportés par les acteurs privés sur une période de 6 ans (2024 - 2029).

L'objectif est d'accélérer l’innovation, développer et diversifier les solutions de biocontrôle et de biostimulation disponible, en combinaison avec d’autres solutions, pour une protection agroécologique des plantes.  

L'association engage et fédère les acteurs de l’amont à l’aval dans l’innovation et la transition agroécologique: structures de l’enseignement et de la recherche, entreprises du biocontrôle et de la biostimulation, entreprises du numérique, obtenteurs, instituts techniques agricoles, prestataires de l’expérimentation, filières de production agricole et acteurs de leur aval de la transformation à la distribution, investisseurs, consultants, pôles de compétitivité, représentants des consommateurs et citoyens, etc.

Name: PRE - Côte d'Ivoire
Description:
For contributing to the global fight against climate change, and in light of the challenges posed to its economy by the deterioration of its forest cover, Côte d'Ivoire signed up to the international Reducing Emissions from Deforestation and forest Degradation (REDD+) mechanism in 2011.
This mechanism aims to support and reward countries for their efforts to preserve and restore their forest capital as part of a transition to a more sustainable development model that is resilient to climate change.

The effective implementation of the mechanism since 2014 has led in 2020, to the signing of Greenhouse Gas, Emission Reduction Payment Agreements (ERPAs) resulting from the implementation of activities under the Emission Reduction Payment around Tai National Park (ERP) project.
The ERP project is part of the third phase of the REDD+ mechanism, namely the Payment for Results phase, and aims to value 10MtCO2eq reduced between October 2020 (date of signature of the agreements) and December 31, 2024.
The project entered into force in February 2022, after the country had met all the conditions for entry into force described in the ERPA, namely, (i) submission of the letter of approval, (ii) submission of evidence demonstrating the ability of the Ministry of Economy and Finance (MEF) to transfer carbon credits, (iii)

Name: WasteFree23
Description:
Waste Free in ‘23 is an organization that employs innovative, new recycling process to make the world waste free by working with communities around the world. 

Waste Free in ’23 is unique because it:

1 Recycles 100% of plastic waste in an environmentally-friendly manner
2 Recycles waste at locally-built micro-recycling centers located in low-income communities
3 Recycles waste directly at the source
4 Eliminates the need for transportation between collection, dumps, and recycling plants
5 Creates jobs for local members of the community
6 Provides a source of income through products built from recycled plastic

At the beginning of January 2023, Slums Going Green and Clean (SGGC), Kenya began recycling 200kg of plastic waste per day in one micro-recycling center, and will increase their recycling capacity to 15 micro-recycling centers recycling 3 tons of waste per day in Kibera by the end of 2023.

We hope to spread the Waste Free in ’23 Initiative worldwide!

Name: Blue Azores
Description:
O Blue Azores nasce em 2019 através de uma parceria entre o Governo Regional dos Açores, a Fundação Oceano Azul e o Instituto Waitt, que se uniram em torno de uma visão comum - 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗴𝗲𝗿, 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗼𝘃𝗲𝗿 𝗲 𝘃𝗮𝗹𝗼𝗿𝗶𝘇𝗮𝗿 𝗼 𝗰𝗮𝗽𝗶𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗻𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗵𝗼 𝗱𝗼𝘀 𝗔𝗰̧𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀 - que suporta uma ambição de garantir um oceano saudável como base de uma economia azul próspera e sustentável.

O programa assenta nos seguintes objetivos principais:

- Proteger 30% do Mar dos Açores através da implementação efetiva da Rede de Áreas Marinhas Protegidas dos Açores (RAMPA).
- Rever e integrar as áreas marinhas protegidas costeiras na RAMPA.
- Assegurar a sustentabilidade financeira e a gestão eficaz do Parque Marinho dos Açores.
- Apoiar medidas de reestruturação do setor da pesca.

 Blue Azores was born in 2019 from a partnership between the Regional Government of the Azores, the Oceano Azul Foundation and the Waitt Institute, which came together around a common vision - to 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁, 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗼𝘁𝗲 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘃𝗮𝗹𝘂𝗲 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗺𝗮𝗿𝗶𝗻𝗲 𝗻𝗮𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗮𝗹 𝗰𝗮𝗽𝗶𝘁𝗮𝗹 𝗼𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗔𝘇𝗼𝗿𝗲𝘀 - which supports an ambition to ensure a healthy ocean as the basis of a prosperous and sustainable blue economy.

The program is built upon the following main objectives:

- Protect 30% of the Azores Sea through the implementation of the Azores MPA Network.
- Review and integrate coastal marine protected areas into the Azores MPA Network. 
- Ensure the financial sustainability and effective management of the Azores Marine Park.
- Support the restructuring of the fisheries sector.

Name: PAP/RAC - UNEP/MAP
Description:
PAP/RAC STORY
Established in 1977, PAP/RAC is one the six Regional Activity Centres (RACs) acting within the Mediterranean Action Plan (MAP) of the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP). 

https://linktr.ee/pap_rac

Based in Split (Croatia), PAP/RAC's multidisciplinary professionals co-operate with the governments’ appointed representatives, international and national institutions, including a wide network of experts and stakeholders. Our operational activities are funded by the Contracting Parties of the Barcelona Convention through the Mediterranean Trust Fund. In addition, we mobilize funds from different donors, such as the Global Environment Facility (GEF) and the European Union (EU).   

PAP/RAC's MISSION
In order to tackle the complex environmental, social, economic and institutional challenges the coastal zones are continuously facing, we work together with the Mediterranean countries towards the implementation of the Protocol on Integrated Coastal Zone Management. 

In our work we apply the integrated coastal zone manamenet (ICZM). The ICZM process involves different sectors, levels of government, civil society and coastal communities, with the aim to minimise negative environmental impacts, manage coastal resources in a sustainable way and improve the quality of life in the Mediterranean. 

WHY IS THE ICZM PROTOCOL IMPORTANT?
This Protocol, signed in Madrid in 2008, is the first international legally binding instrument on ICZM.
From coastal ecosystems and landscapes, wetlands and estuaries, marine habitats, coastal forests, woods to dunes and beaches - the ICZM Protocol supports the Mediterranean countries to better manage and protect their coastal zones and to deal with climate change.

TOGETHER WITH MEDITERRANEAN COUNTRIES, PAP/RAC WORK TOWARDS: 
* RESILIENT
* HEALTY
* PRODUCTIVE
* DISTINCTIVE 
* DIVERSE and
* ATTRACTIVE 

MEDITERRANEAN COASTAL ZONE!

Name: Institut Bauen und Umwelt e.V.
Description:
IBU was created out of an initiative of manufacturers of construction products and components who decided to support the demand for more sustainability in the construction sector.

The focus is on convincing everyone that sustainability is the right choice. IBU´s environmental product declarations (EPDs) were created in close cooperation with construction and environmental authorities in Germany and international standardization processes.

IBU is currently the only organization in Germany that verified consistently based on the international standards ISO14025 and EN 15804 for all kinds of construction products. In addition to manufacturers, independent experts from research, Germany´s Ministry of Construction, the German Environmental Agency (UBA), and health and environmental experts are involved in audits. The IBU EPD provides a lot of information, credibility, and acceptance. 

The institute’s over 240 members, from 22 different countries, from SMEs to global corporations, from more than 40 associations and over 100 different product categories, exemplify the enormous variety of products and branches that rely on EPDs to provide a neutral, independent, proven, and comprehensive information tool for detailing the environmental effects of products.

Name: Seabin Foundation
Description:
Seabin Foundation is an independent not-for-profit entity that operates in parallel with SeabinTM. Seabin Foundation’s mission statement is to educate, advocate, and disseminate information about litter prevention and oceanic plastic pollution through science and community engagement to help drive environmental stewardship and create more responsible consumption and legislation towards single-use and other problematic plastics found in Seabins, typically located in urban waterways.
 
In 2022, Seabin Foundation launched a solar-powered microplastics research laboratory, the Ocean Health Lab (OHL), built from a retrofitted 40ft shipping container. The OHL is located at the Australian National Maritime Museum (Darling Harbour, Sydney) and operates as a functioning research facility as well as an educative exhibition for guests of the museum to visit and learn about ocean plastics.

In 2024, Seabin Foundation launched a FREE 7-module Ocean Health online course, to reach national and global audiences regardless of their location near and far the oceans and Seabins. The 4-hour interactive video & quiz content was developed, thanks to valuable collaborations, to increase ocean literacy and inspire positive actions such as conscious decision-making, volunteering, and considering a career in ocean conservation.

Name: Stimular
Description:
Stimular is a foundation that aims to accelerate sustainable business.

Most of Stimular its activities are located within the Netherlands. However, Stimular also creates incidental partnerships with knowledge institutions and universities to share (and receive) knowledge, for example in Denmark and Suriname. Furthermore, the Envirometer is also used by international companies. Therefore, the Envirometer is also available in English.

Stimular answers the growing need for sustainability with practical instruments for SME, branch associations, governmental organisations and healthcare. Sustainability is therefore embedded into the core strategies and mission of Stimular. The goal is that organisations and managers include sustainability in every decision they make. Through the websites Stimular shares its advice and experience to help companies implement sustainability into their business.

Stimular helps organisations by giving advice adapted to a specific situation, communicating in a clear way and creating inspiring contacts with other companies. The aim with the clients is not just to fulfil the needed requirements, but to embed sustainability into their way of doing business. Stimular supports companies by assisting them with activities such as:

Carbon Footprints
Setting up energy saving plans
Analysing the supply chain
Assisting in the process to obtain sustainability certificates
Stimular also works with governmental organisations and branch associations. By doing so, Stimular is able to reach out to many organisations interested in implementing sustainability, but lack the information to know how. Companies can make a fluent start with the practical and accessible instruments, like the Envirometer. This tool enables companies to visualise their achievements in sustainability, including a carbon footprint, fast and easy. Stimular also develops scans for specific industry sectors and creates certificates proving the organisation has a sustainable business.

Name: Demeter France
Description:
Le label Demeter est l'un des plus exigeants au monde, complémentaire à la règlementation bio européenne. Il s’appuie sur une approche holistique de l’agriculture, du respect du vivant et de la qualité alimentaire, cosmétique et textile. 

Prendre soin de la Terre, des écosystèmes et développer des fermes plus résilientes permet la production de matières premières nutritives ainsi que la pleine expression des différents terroirs.

Pour nous, la qualité est définie par la façon dont les ingrédients ont été cultivés puis préservés lors de leur transformation, et par l’utilisation ou non de substances problématiques. C’est pour cela que nos cahiers des charges sont très exigeants et complets : exigences les plus élevées pour la production agricole, process de transformation encadrés, refus de nombreux additifs, exigences pour les emballages et la responsabilité sociale, sociétale et écologique. Par ailleurs, nos cahiers des charges s’inscrivent dans une démarche de progrès et sont régulièrement complétés.

Demeter labellise aujourd’hui près de 9000 fermes et entreprises dans le monde, dans plus de 60 pays. 

En France, comme dans les principaux pays où il est présent, le label Demeter bénéficie d’un très bon taux de confiance* auprès des consommateurs de produits biologiques - et la démarche Demeter est aujourd’hui identifiée comme l’une des plus exigeantes**, avec une notoriété grandissante.***

 *sources : étude Opinion Way pour le Cluster Bio, 2021; étude Biotopia pour Demeter, 2023
**sources : étude Le Basic pour Greenpeace, 2021
*** source : La grande enquête annuelle du bio, étude Biotopia, 2023

Name: Viridi Air
Description:
Innovatief & 100% duurzaam initiatief voor gezondheid en klimaat. Viridi Air plant namens bedrijven & particulieren inheemse luchtzuiverende bomen (BBA-aanplantwijze) die CO2 maar ook fijnstof en andere schadelijke stoffen uit de buitenlucht filteren. Schone lucht, voor gezondheid en klimaat! Schone Lucht-certificaat, Schone Lucht-label, Marketing Exposure/promotie en Netwerkclub. Meedoen vanaf €5,- (ex btw) per boom. 

Draag ook bij aan gezondheid en klimaat
De buitenlucht is vervuild (o.a. fijnstof) waardoor in Nederland en België jaarlijks 20.000 mensen overlijden. Helaas komt vervuilde buitenlucht ook in de binnenlucht en warmt de aarde op door een hoge uitstoot aan CO2. Viridi Air lost dit op door de BBA-aanplantwijze en gebruik van unieke (inheemse) luchtzuiverende bomen in door Viridi Air geselecteerde aanplantlocaties. Beter voor het klimaat, luchtkwaliteit en gezondheid. Beschikbare samenwerkingsvormen:

- Viridi Air Partnership (incl. standaard & extra marketing)
- Bomen laten planten (incl. standaard marketing)
- Doneren
- Geef een boom cadeau
- Teamuitje, zelf bomen planten

Name: aDryada
Description: aDryada develops and operates large-scale (>50,000 ha) nature-based projects (forest, mangrove and wetland, etc.) focusing on biodiversity and maximizing positive social impacts, thereby generating high-quality carbon credits. Working closely with governments as well as leading global financial institutions and corporations, aDryada aims to generate 400 MT of carbon equivalent. over the next 30 years to be sold on the international carbon credit market. The company is also a co-founder of the Organization for Biodiversity Certificates, aiming to generate the first biodiversity certificates, valuing nature-based solutions projects.

Name: Sustainable Textiles Switzerland
Description:
Sustainable Textiles Switzerland 2030 (STS 2030) ist ein Multi-Stakeholder-Programm mit der Mission, einen wesentlichen Beitrag zur Erreichung der Nachhaltigen Entwicklungsziele (SDGs) im Schweizer Textil- und Bekleidungssektor entlang der gesamten Wertschöpfungskette zu leisten.

***

Sustainable Textiles Switzerland 2030 (STS 2030) est un programme multipartite ayant pour mission d’apporter une contribution significative à la réalisation des Objectifs de développement durable (ODD) dans le secteur suisse du textile et de l’habillement tout au long de la chaîne de création de valeur.

***

STS 2030 is a multi-stakeholder program with the mission to contribute significantly to the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) in the Swiss textile and clothing sector along the entire value chain.

Name: Klimaatverbond Nederland
Description:
Sinds 1992 bereiden we samen met gemeenten, provincies, waterschappen, het Rijk en inhoudelijke experts de wegen richting effectief lokaal klimaatbeleid. 

Wij focussen hierbij op twee domeinen: 
- Mitigatie: het voorkomen van verdere klimaatverandering door reductie van de uitstoot van broeikasgassen. 
- Adaptatie: het aanpassen aan de veranderingen die we nu en in de toekomst ondervinden.

We bewegen mee met de actualiteit en zetten onze ervaring en kennis in om lokale overheden aan passend klimaatbeleid te helpen. 

Dit gaat om beleid op thema’s die nu aandacht vragen: energietransitie, hitte, koelte, CO2-reductie en meer. We maken de weg vrij voor kansrijke oplossingsrichtingen en beleidsinstrumenten.

Sluit je aan bij het verbond door lid te worden van Klimaatverbond Nederland. Informeer via deze link naar de waarde van het lidmaatschap. https://klimaatverbond.nl/lid-worden/

The Net Zero Carbon program provides partners a roadmap to achieve carbon neutrality by sharing best practices, scaling up carbon emission reduction and tracking, and providing better access to green financing, among many other capacity-building tools.

For too long, Baltimore’s waterways have been dumping grounds for contaminated stormwater runoff, trash, sewage, and toxic chemicals. This pollution not only harms our environment, it also threatens our citizens’ health and the vitality of our local economy.

Blue Water Baltimore fills a unique role in the Baltimore area as an organization that uses a holistic set of programs to meet the great challenges of improving water quality. From arts and education, to stormwater management projects, to legal and legislative advocacy, we bring together citizens and our public and private partners to accomplish more than any of us could do alone.

Name: The International Commission for the Protection of the Waters of Lake Geneva - CIPEL
Description:
The CIPEL - Franco-Swiss cooperation for the water of Lake Geneva

The International Commission for the Protection of the Waters of Lake Geneva (CIPEL),
an intergovernmental Franco-Swiss body, has been contributing since 1963 to the coordination of water policy on the scale of the Lake Geneva basin, i.e. between France and Switzerland, and more particularly on the territories of the departments of Ain and Haute-Savoie as well as the cantons of Vaud, Valais and Geneva.

Its perimeter, covering an area of more than 10,000 km2 , covers the catchment area of the Lake Geneva and, more generally, of the Rhône upstream of the Franco-Swiss border.

Rims Nature Limited is a Kenya-based social enterprise advancing environmental conservation, ecological restoration, and nature-positive innovation. We turn today’s challenges deforestation, waste, food insecurity, biodiversity loss into opportunities for green livelihoods. By blending indigenous knowledge, practical science, and entrepreneurship, we design community-led solutions that protect ecosystems while improving household well-being.

Vision
A thriving planet where nature and people coexist in harmony.

Mission
To restore, conserve, and protect natural ecosystems while empowering communities through nature-positive solutions, green innovation, and environmental education.

Core Values
Sustainability: Long-term, regenerative impact.
Integrity: Transparency and accountability.
Innovation: Creative, scalable, tech-enabled solutions.
Community Empowerment: Local leadership and inclusion.
Resilience: People and ecosystems that adapt and thrive.

Focus Areas & Innovations
EcoBriq & Waste-to-Energy: Transforming agricultural and urban organic waste into clean, affordable briquettes cutting reliance on charcoal and firewood, reducing pollution, and advancing a circular economy.
Hydroponics & Tower Gardens: Water-smart, space-efficient food production for homes, schools, and micro-enterprises; higher yields, year-round nutrition, and dignified green jobs.
Biochar Production: Converting crop residues into soil-enhancing biochar that boosts fertility, retains moisture, and stores carbon for the long term.
Beekeeping & Pollinator Conservation: Modern apiculture and habitat stewardship that sustain biodiversity and generate income from honey and beeswax.

Our Approach
We co-create with communities, prioritize youth and women, and use data and GeoIT to target impact hot-spots, monitor outcomes, and scale what works.

Partnerships & Call to Action
We collaborate with public agencies, civil society, research institutions, and mission-aligned investors. Join us to expand...

Name: Syndicat Mixte Baie de Somme Grand Littoral Picard
Description:
Créé en 1974, le Syndicat Mixte Baie de Somme est un établissement public financé principalement par le Conseil départemental de la Somme. Sa mission est de développer et de valoriser le territoire exceptionnel de la Baie de Somme, le littoral picard et son avant-pays à travers la gestion, l’aménagement des espaces naturels et bâtis, et l’optimisation de leurs ressources.

En tant qu'outil stratégique et opérationnel au service des politiques publiques, nous œuvrons également sur des enjeux cruciaux tels que la protection du littoral (trait de côte) et la gestion d'équipements touristiques majeurs. Nos projets phares intègrent les valeurs du label Grand Site de France Baie de Somme, ainsi que les problématiques du Programme d’Actions et de Prévention des Inondations (PAPI).

Nos actions s'articulent autour de trois axes principaux :

    Gestion des espaces et ressources naturels,
    Protection du littoral et anticipation des risques,
    Développement territorial et économique.

Nous avons défini 5 priorités stratégiques pour les années à venir :

    La préservation du Grand Site de France - Baie de Somme,
    Le Programme d'Actions et de Prévention des Inondations (PAPI),
    Le développement des projets de réaménagements communaux (Ault, Cayeux...)
    La consolidation du Plan Vélo Baie de Somme,
    Le démarche responsable de notre régie commerciale Destination Baie de Somme.

Nos infrastructures touristiques emblématiques incluent :

    L'Aquaclub de Belle-Dune,
    Les Jardins de Valloires,
    Le Golf de Belle-Dune,
    L'Hôtel & Restaurant Cap Hornu,
    La Maison de la Baie de Somme,
    Le Parc du Marquenterre,
    Le Musée Picarvie.

Pour en savoir plus, visitez notre site : www.baiedesomme.org
Découvrez Destination Baie de Somme : www.baiedesomme.fr

Past and current methods for dealing with oil spills are not solutions, but they do reduce the negative effects of spills, whereas Z-Enviroil is designed to restore oil spills rather than simply remove them.
Z-Enviroil retrieves oil spills from wet and dry surfaces instantly and helps companies and factories that use oil tanks or pipelines in their industries overcome crises while protecting the environment.

Z-Enviroil provides a full-coverage solution for oil spills, including warning systems, recovery systems, on-site emergency aid services, and insurance programs.

Name: Certified Naturally Grown
Description:
Certified Naturally Grown (CNG) offers peer-review certification to farmers and beekeepers producing food, flowers, and fiber for their local communities by working in harmony with nature, without relying on synthetic chemicals or GMOs.

Our standards are based on the highest ideals of the organic movement. Our approach is based on transparency, direct relationships and a firm belief in our ability to create something uniquely valuable by working together.

Certified Naturally Grown uses a different approach to certification, called a Participatory Guarantee System (PGS). In addition to being more affordable and less reliant on paperwork, PGS are distinguished by their approach, in which inspections are carried out by peers – typically other farmers in the area. This certification model is centered on active participation of farmers and other stakeholders, and built on a foundation of trust, transparency, direct relationships and knowledge exchange.

Participatory Guarantee Systems have existed for decades, and have been recognized for providing an important alternative to third-party certification programs, often more attractive and accessible to direct-market producers. PGS promote organic practices and fostering farmer success by opening doors to new markets. PGS also strengthen the farming community through mutual support and educational opportunities.

IFOAM – Organics International (the International Federation of Organic Agriculture Movements) actively supports the development of PGS with educational materials, the publication of an international PGS directory, and its PGS committee. An estimated fifty thousand farmers in more than 50 countries participate in this type of peer-review certification program. In some countries PGS has gained official government recognition and has entered into organic regulations.

Name: AIRO Network
Description: AIRO Network is a cutting-edge platform pioneering advancements in air quality science and technology. Our mission is to provide accurate, real-time data on critical pollutants such as PM2.5, PM10, Ozone (O₃), Nitrogen Dioxide (NO₂), Carbon Monoxide (CO), and CO₂ to address the escalating health and environmental challenges posed by air pollution and global warming. By leveraging advanced technologies like IoT sensors, blockchain integration, and AI-driven analytics, AIRO Network empowers communities and businesses to make informed decisions toward improving air quality and public health. Our platform uses a decentralized model to enable community involvement through AIRO Monitoring Stations, rewarding contributors who share air quality data with AIRO Credits. This fosters a collaborative network dedicated to environmental health. Our innovative AIROMAP platform provides a comprehensive mapping tool that visualizes air quality trends, offering actionable insights to users and enabling impactful decision-making at both local and global levels. Together, we aim to transform air quality challenges into opportunities for a healthier future.

Name: BIOservicES
Description:
Soil organisms play a crucial role in many of the processes that occur in soil, which are directly linked to the provision of ecosystem services. While it's clear that soil organisms are essential for providing multiple ecosystem services, we still need to explore the relationships between specific soil organisms and the provision of essential ecosystem services across various types of land use. In addition, there is a lack of information on how nutrient cycling, pollution prevention, water infiltration/retention, productivity, soil structure, and soil biodiversity are related to different land uses. 

Using a statistical approach with cutting-edge elements from AI, such as statistical learning and machine learning algorithms, BIOservicES will establish the relationship between soil organisms, and their multiple uses. The primary goal of BIOservicES is to comprehend the connection between various soil organisms such as viruses, bacteria, archaea, fungi, protists, nematodes, microarthropods, earthworms, isopods, millipedes, insects, and spiders, and how they contribute to the delivery of multiple soil ecosystem functions and services at different scales, taking into account different land uses and climate change. The project seeks to identify the pressures and drivers affecting soil organisms and their services, perform an economic valuation of their contribution to the ecosystem, and develop decision-support tools and models that can help design climate-resilient management practices and monitoring, conservation, and restoration programs.

The 60-month project, funded by the European Union through the Horizon Europe program, will involve 22 partners from 11 countries with very different profiles (universities, small and medium-sized enterprises, consultancies, farmers, and NGOs, among others).

Name: ARPROE - Action pour la Régénération en Plongée des Récifs d'Ostrea Edulis.
Description:
The regeneration of ostrea edulis (flat oysters) reefs is a priority identified by OSPAR*. Ostrea edulis is listed as a species at risk of extinction. 

ARPROE regenerates ostrea edulis reefs.

The flat oyster is an ingenious species that builds reefs under the foreshore, its habitat. These reefs act on our coasts like corals in tropical zones, but they have virtually disappeared. 

- These reefs encourage the development of significant biodiversity, providing habitat, protection and food. 
- These reefs increase the roughness of the seabed, allow CO2-capturing algae to cling on and help to protect the coastline. 
- Clinging to the reefs, flat oysters filter the water beyond the foreshore. They are sentinels, indicating the state of health of coastal ecosystems.
- The regeneration of flat oyster reefs, harvested by diving in a rational manner, contributes to food safety. 
- Oysters are a healthy food, rich in proteins and nutrients that promote good human health. 

Today, flat oysters are too often found rolling around on the seabed or too sparse to allow the species to regenerate its habitat on its own. Scattered and not fixed, they are more vulnerable to epizootics and predators. 

In the 1970s, two epizootics, Bonamia and Marteilia, almost wiped out ostrea edulis, which were already victims of overfishing. 
The Institut Français de Recherche pour l'Exploitation de la Mer or Ifremer's Forever project showed that it was possible to regenerate ostrea edulis reefs. 

ARPROE's action is based on three pillars: 
- Regenerating ostrea edulis reefs as a catalyst for biodiversity. 
- Providing information and training to enable everyone to participate in this regeneration. 
- Develop dive fishing, to offer a professional life outdoors, while having a positive impact on the environment. 

*OSPAR: Convention for the Protection of the Marine Environment of the North-East Atlantic

Description:

ES šalys yra pasiryžusios iki 2050 m. užtikrinti, kad ES poveikis klimatui būtų neutralus. Europos Vadovų Tarybos 2020 m. gruodžio 10-11 d. išvadose patvirtintas 2030 m. klimato ir energetikos politikos tikslas – sumažinti išmetamų ŠESD kiekį ne mažiau kaip 55 proc., palyginti su 1990 m., bei parengti vadinamąjį pasirengimo įgyvendinti 55 % tikslą priemonių rinkinį. Remiantis Europos Vadovų Tarybos išvadomis, 2020 m. gruodžio 17 d. Aplinkos taryba pritarė Europos klimato reglamentui. Perėjimas prie netaršios visuomenės ir ekonomikos yra neatidėliotinas uždavinys, atsižvelgiant į augantį ekstremaliųjų meteorologinių reiškinių skaičių, tai yra ir galimybę kurti naujas darbo vietas bei ekonominę naudą. Žaliasis kursas yra būtinas žingsnis siekiant padidinti ES energetinę nepriklausomybę, o iškastinį kurą pakeitus atsinaujinančių išteklių energija sumažės ES išmetamas ŠESD kiekis. ES misijos – Europos Komisijos koordinuojami veiksmai, kurių paskirtis yra sutelkti reikiamus išteklius: finansavimo programas, politinius sprendimus ir teisinį reglamentavimą, konkretiems strateginiams tikslams pasiekti. Šiuo atveju siekiama paspartinti miestų, jau žengiančių CO2 neutralumo link, žingsnius ir įtraukti naujus miestus, kurie keltų neutralumo tikslą.

Name: Klimatická koalice
Description:
Klimatická koalice je platformou spojující české nevládní organizace a iniciativy, kterým záleží na klimatu. Podporujeme spolupráci, vytváříme prostor pro otevřenou diskusi a zvyšujeme povědomí o nutnosti okamžitých a spravedlivých řešení klimatické krize.

Vznikli jsme v roce 2007 jako neoficiální uskupení, v roce 2025 jsme se transformovali na zapsaný spolek.

Poslání:
- SPOLUVYTVÁŘET A POSILAT širší klimatické hnutí, podporovat jeho vzájemnou i mezioborovou spolupráci a sebevzdělávání
- VYTVÁŘET BEZPEČNÝ PROSTOR pro otevřenou diskusi, sdílení informací, znalostí a zkušeností mezi členskými a přidruženými organizacemi
- ZVYŠOVAT POVĚDOMÍ o klimatické krizi jako o naléhavém problému, který vyžaduje bezodkladná řešení, a oponovat environmentálně a sociálně nespravedlivým řešením

Naše členské organizace se zabývají zejména:
1. Ochranou životního prostředí – především ve vztahu ke klimatické krizi, biodiverzitě a znečištění antropogenními chemickými látkami
2. Komunikací a vedením veřejné diskuse o klimatické krizi
3. Rozvojovou spoluprací a humanitární pomocí v kontextu dopadu klimatické krize
4. Prosazováním komplexních udržitelných, sociálně spravedlivých a environmentálně příznivých řešení
5. Environmentálním vzděláváním a osvětou k výše uvedenému

V letech 2023–2025 koordinujeme projekt Zdraví a klimatická změna, který usiluje o zvýšení klimatické gramotnosti českého zdravotnictví: https://klimatickakoalice.cz/projekty/zdravi-a-klimaticka-zmena

Name: Vale da Esteva
Description:
VALE DA ESTEVA - Um Legado para a Vida

O Vale da Esteva situa-se no Parque Natural do Tejo Internacional, concelho de Castelo Branco, junto às margens do Rio Ponsul (primeiro afluente do Rio Tejo em Portugal, que se destaca pela riqueza e diversidade da flora e fauna).
Com uma área de 104,9 hectares, o Vale da Esteva distingue-se por uma paisagem onde a natureza se encontra no seu estado selvagem e puro. O vasto e diverso conjunto de espécies florestais, florísticas e aromáticas confere a esta região um valor ecológico inestimável.

Visão
“Um Legado Para a Vida”
Implementar um Santuário Ecológico no Vale da Esteva.
O Vale da Esteva é um lugar onde as relações entre as pessoas e das pessoas com os animais e a natureza, têm como primado a busca constante de integridade, harmonia e compaixão, em prol do bem-estar de todos os seres vivos. 
Acreditamos que a harmonia das relações humanas depende de uma maior harmonia nas relações com o mundo animal e que deve ter por base o respeito pela dignidade de todas as espécies. 
Seguindo práticas sustentáveis e da economia circular, pretendemos inspirar e motivar as pessoas para a preservação dos ecossistemas e a salvaguarda dos direitos dos seres humanos e dos animais.

Valores
Os nossos valores têm por base a ética ecológica, bem como a valorização e a dignificação da vida, com base na inclusão, na transparência, na cooperação, na responsabilidade, na integridade e na inovação. 

Missão
Contribuirmos ativamente para a preservação dos ecossistemas e deixarmos – Um Legado para a Vida.

Name: Puget Soundkeeper
Description:
Puget Soundkeeper’s mission is to protect and enhance the waters of Puget Sound.

We monitor Puget Sound water quality, help set strong policies and regulations that protect our waterways and our health, enforce environmental regulations, engage citizens and businesses in waterway cleanups and recovery projects, educate and involve the public in local water pollution issues, and partner with local and regional groups to advance solutions that protect Puget Sound.

Monitoring and Enforcement: Soundkeeper actively patrols the waters of Puget Sound and monitors for pollution. We also enforce the Clean Water Act through legal action.

The Clean Water Act of 1972 grants individuals and communities the power to sue under provisions of the Act to bring egregious polluters into compliance with the law. Soundkeeper has a nearly 100% success record in Clean Water Act cases and has filed over 170 cases. A 1993 settlement with the City of Bremerton is directly attributable to the Dyes Inlet shellfish beds reopening for the first time in 40 years. On average, Soundkeeper’s settlements control over 120 million gallons of stormwater annually. 

To date, Soundkeeper’s enforcement team has awarded over $7.4 million to third party restoration, education and water quality mitigation projects to heal the damage in the affected watershed and provide an incentive for future compliance. Soundkeeper does not receive any settlement money from Clean Water Act cases.

Policy and Civic Engagement: We pursue the Clean Water Act’s goals through active engagement with business, government agencies and Puget Sound communities.

Stewardship and Education: Soundkeeper is committed to stopping pollution at the source. We partner with businesses to help reduce pollution and host cleanup and education events to involve community members in caring for Puget Sound.

Soundkeeper represents our members as we work to protect our shared waters. Become a member today at www.pugetsoundkeeper.org.

Name: ARTPORT_making waves
Description:
ARTPORT_making waves started as a curatorial collective at the intersection of art, climate change and sustainability in New York, Valencia, Spain in 2006 to draw attention to the causes, consequences, and possible solutions to climate change through themed exhibitions, film productions, workshops, conferences and educational programs.

Today we are a network of international curators and collaborators in different parts of the world and connect interdisciplinary projects through the arts to science and sustainable development. Artists and curators are our protagonists, but scientists and policymakers have become important stakeholders in our work because our final goal has shifted from mere awareness-raising to contributing to real change.

We started with sensitization and are today strong advocates for solutions. These solutions can only be pushed through following different agendas. Our focus on collaborating with scientific institutions, supranational organisations, museums, educational entities, international conferences, governments and private foundations is helping us to diversify these agendas and to contribute to a society which takes sustainable and ecological development seriously.

ARTPORT's Co-founding Executive Director Anne-Marie Melster launched, in 2019, the WE ARE OCEAN Global Program: WE ARE OCEAN is an interdisciplinary art project which gathers artists, students, scientists, policy makers, art collectors, teachers, and curators in order
to raise awareness and engage in dialogue about the environmental condition of the ocean and the role humans play in its current and future state.
The project investigates how we interact with the ocean and how interdependent humans and the ocean are. The overall goal is to raise scientific and
political awareness through the arts, particularly among young people, to stimulate behavioural change and social action and help them to act responsibly
and become conscientious citizens.

Name: SWEET Lantern

Name: IQSW - Indice de Qualité des Sols Wallons
Description:
L’IQSW est un outil en cours de développement.
A terme, il devrait permettre de diagnostiquer de la qualité d’un sol, conçu en concertation avec les acteurs de terrain, pour orienter l’utilisation du sol en phase avec son état de qualité actuel, à destination de tout acteur public ou privé qui exploite, utilise, protège, restaure (voire crée) les sols.

L'IQSW se déclinera en 2 volets : 

 - L'IQSW-citoyen qui sera spécialement conçu pour tout particulier souhaitant diagnostiquer la qualité du sol de son jardin, de son potager grâce à des tests de terrain simples.

 - L'IQSW-pro qui aura pour objectif de décrire la qualité d’un sol, quel que soit son usage, en s’appuyant sur une série de tests de terrain et d’analyses de sol. Son but étant d'orienter l’utilisation du sol en phase avec son état de qualité actuel, notamment à travers une meilleure prise en compte des sols dans les études d’incidences environnementales.

Intéressé par le projet ? Contactez-nous via l'adresse contact@iqsw.be !

Un projet financé par le Plan de Relance de la Wallonie et porté par l'UCLouvain, le Centre wallon de Recherches agronomiques (CRA-W) et le bureau d'études ARIES Consultants.

Name: Lipu ODV - BirdLife Italia
Description:
La Lipu è tra le associazioni ambientaliste più importanti d'Italia e d'Europa.

Fondata nel 1965, ha contribuito alla creazione di oasi, parchi, riserve naturali, alla designazione e protezione della rete Natura 2000 in Italia, alla salvezza di specie di uccelli minacciate tra cui la cicogna bianca, il falco della Regina, il grillaio, alla nascita dell’antibracconaggio e alla diffusione del birdwatching nel nostro paese.

Oggi la Lipu conta 35.000 sostenitori, circa 100 delegazioni e gruppi locali e 1500 volontari attivi sul territorio.

Gestisce 29 oasi e riserve naturali e 9 centri recupero fauna selvatica. Svolge progetti nazionali e internazionali e attività di protezione degli uccelli, conservazione della biodiversità, politiche ambientali, educazione ambientale. Promuove la conoscenza della natura e la cultura ecologica attraverso pubblicazioni, documenti, attività di formazione, eventi.

La Lipu è un ente morale riconosciuto dal Presidente della Repubblica, un’associazione ambientalista riconosciuta dal ministero dell’Ambiente, un'Organizzazione di volontariato, un ente in grado di svolgere ricerca scientifica, un membro dell’Iucn (International Union for Conservation of Nature).

La Lipu è il partner italiano di BirdLife International, la più grande federazione internazionale per la protezione degli uccelli, con 121 membri in tutto il mondo.

Gli uccelli sono il simbolo e l’orizzonte della Lipu. La loro presenza, i canti, i voli, i colori rappresentano la bellissima speranza di un mondo migliore.

Name: Wad Gaat Om
Description: De vervuiling door plasticafval in de natuur bedreigt de flora en fauna in het Waddengebied. Het Waddengebied is uniek in haar soort en je vindt er talloze planten en dieren. Vervuiling door plastic afval is een grote bedreiging voor het leven op het wad. Met Wad gaat Om werken we aan een integrale systeemaanpak om de plasticvervuiling in het Waddengebied tegen te gaan. We zetten in op een aanpak vanaf de bron. Dus schoon maken én schoon houden. Je kunt daarbij denken aan acties die zich richten op opruimen van afval, afvangen via rivieren, preventie en reductie via inkoop en het ontwikkelen van alternatieven voor verpakkingsmaterialen.

Name: Saltwater (LME RETAIL GmbH)

sandige füße und salzige haare

Verbunden durch die Liebe zum Meer
Unsere Leidenschaft gilt dem Surf-, Vanlife- und Adventure-Lifestyle – und diese spiegelt sich in unserem ausgewählten Sortiment wider.

Gegründet wurde Saltwater im Jahr 2021 von einer Gruppe leidenschaftlicher Abenteurer*innen, die ihren eigenen Lifestyle leben und lieben. Die Gründer haben Saltwater aus ihrer Begeisterung für das Leben am und mit dem Meer ins Leben gerufen und seitdem stetig weiterentwickelt.

Die Auswahl der touristischen Standorte wie Sylt oder Fehmarn ist kein Zufall: Hier treffen die Welten von Surf, Vanlife und Adventure aufeinander und bieten den perfekten Rahmen für unsere Stores.

Wir legen großen Wert auf Nachhaltigkeit und bieten Produkte an, die im Einklang mit der Natur stehen. Von umweltfreundlichen Surfboards über Outdoor-Equipment bis hin zu nachhaltigen Vanlife-Gadgets – bei uns finden Abenteurer*innen alles, was sie für ein Leben in und mit der Natur benötigen.

Ein besonderes Highlight ist unsere eigene Produktreihe, die wir mit viel Herzblut und Sorgfalt entwickelt haben. Dazu gehören unter anderem unser hochwertiges Olivenöl, Meersalz, Kaffee sowie eine exklusive Auswahl an Handtüchern, Bechern, Hoodies und T-Shirts. Diese Kollektion wird in Zukunft weiter ausgebaut und steht für die hohe Qualität und Authentizität, die unsere Marke ausmacht.

Name: Startup Plastic Surgery
Description:
Startup Plastic Surgery, launched by KIK (Klímainnovációs Közösség - Climate Innovation Community), is the first startup programme in Hungary to offer support to startups aiming to rethink the use of plastics and tackling plastic challenges.

Do you have an innovative solution for the usage or the substitution of plastic?
In the Startup Plastic Surgery programme we work together with innovators who have a feasible solution for the environmental and health problems caused by plastic. We support these ideas and solutions with industry specific mentoring and business coaching in their development into a market-ready enterprise.

We are looking for ideas offering innovative solutions in the following areas:
•	Substitution materials for plastic e.g.: new sustainable packages.
•	Technological innovation especially in the catering and food industry.
•	System innovation and business model innovation through the production, recycling and re-use cycle of plastic.
•	Initiatives encouraging behavioural changes in plastic use.

Name: Itoco Inc.
Description: Our mission is to create a sustainable global carbon sequestration market based on a Removal Unit Program (RMU) – that also helps local communities by creating self-sustaining programs in agriculture, clean energy and food security. ITOCO Inc. trades on the OTC Markets, symbol: ITMC.  ITOCO is a 14-year-old publicly quoted specialty Bio Tech development, production and distribution company based in Toronto Canada and Nevada USA.  Itoco seeks to partner with outstanding individuals and companies within this field to joint venture, research, and co-develop Bio Tech related products and technologies to the market.

Name: Institute for Carbon Removal Law and Policy
Description: The Institute for Carbon Removal Law and Policy is a research center in the School of International Service at American University, Washington DC. We are dedicated to the assessment and promotion of sustainable carbon removal.

Name: Liberian Youth for Climate Actions
Description:
LYCA is a registered not-for-profit NGO with the government of the Republic of Liberia, accredited by the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) of Liberia, and has been operational since January 2020. LYCA's thematic focus areas include climate education, policy advocacy, climate justice, and nature-based solutions. 

Our Vision

We envision a safe and habitable planet for the present and future generations.

Our Mission

We empower youth, women, and vulnerable communities to take action toward a sustainable future through capacity building, promotion of equitable climate policy, and research advocacy.

Name: UNITED BLUERISE
Description: United Bluerise promotes sustainable solutions in our ever changing world.

Name: SûrQual Plus CCE
Description:
un bureau d’études spécialisé en qualité, hygiène, sécurité et environnement (QHSE).
Nous sommes engagé dans la conception de plans QHSE, l'accompagnement à la certification, et les inspections techniques d'équipements soumis à la réglementation. Opérant à l'échelle nationale, notre équipe d’experts est entièrement dédiée aux enjeux du développement durable, de la transition énergétique et environnementale. Nous offrons à nos clients des solutions conformes aux normes strictes en matière de qualité, d'hygiène, de santé, de sécurité et d'environnement. En accord avec les régulations en vigueur, nous collaborons avec particuliers, associations, professionnels, entreprises et collectivités territoriales pour concevoir des projets d’aménagement limitant leur impact QHSE.

Name: Movimento Bora Reciclar!
Description:
Movimento Bora Reciclar! nasceu em 2019, É dedicado à educação e conscientização ambiental, com um foco especial na reciclagem de materiais, principalmente o PET. 

Através de iniciativas como palestras, oficinas e ações interativas, o movimento busca ensinar a importância da separação correta dos resíduos e sua reciclagem. Utilizando plataformas digitais, redes sociais, assessoria de imprensa, programas educativos em escolas e participações em eventos, promovemos o engajamento em práticas sustentáveis. Nosso objetivo é causar um impacto positivo e duradouro na preservação do meio ambiente, garantindo um planeta mais saudável e sustentável para as próximas gerações.

Name: PHCC
Description:
We are a not-for-profit, community based Natural Resource Management organisation that promotes an integrated approach to catchment management and the way we protect and restore the environment within the Peel-Harvey catchment.

With a strong emphasis on partnerships, we work with landholders, community groups, industry, the Australian Government, Government of Western Australia and local governments to affect change ‘on-ground’ and in the way we manage our environment.

Our activities address sustainable natural resource management (NRM) including climate change, river and wetland restoration, biodiversity protection, sustainable agriculture and building community capacity. The past ecological collapse of the Peel-Harvey Estuarine System demands a continued emphasis on water quality issues.

Name: VULKAN Energy
Description:
A Venture company investing in sustainable building materials such as decking planks, 2x4s, sheets, roof tiles and pavement blocks. The products are made with hard to recyclable multi-layer plastic waste, such as ramen packaging and other shit-house single use products.

Our GameFi division Planet Vulkan, is an FPS mobile game on the Polygon chain that will allow gamers in Indonesia to be directly involved in helping us remove plastic waste from nature by leveraging NFTs, Staking and a unique P2E ecosystem. Play the game, remove plastic waste. Currently in development.

Name: NARA-SK
Description:
Welcome to our LinkedIn! Many of you already know us, and for those who don't, let us introduce our work. The National Recycling Agency of Slovakia (NARA-SK) is an environmental non-governmental organization that operates on the principle of the quadruple helix model of collaboration in the social environment. Our vision is the transition to a circular economy in all areas of life as a tool for achieving an environmentally and socially sustainable environment.

At the national level, we influence local policies in the creation of territorial development plans for individual regions. On the international level, we are members of several consortiums of European projects, namely Erasmus+ in the framework of education, the Interreg scheme for the section of sustainable energy, and also Horizon Europe for the creation of new solutions in the textile industry.

We are open to cooperation on any topics related to the circular economy or innovative solutions that contribute to a sustainable environment. Here we will share the results of our work, but also news from our areas of interest as an inspiration for everyone who is interested. Let´s keep in touch!

We focus on quality environment management reduced carbon in the ecosystem, green energy in lighting and cooking .consultancy on the carbon funding.

Description:
Entdecken Sie Innovation und Nachhaltigkeit im Batteriemanagement – mit der Green Belt GmbH

Seit ihrer Gründung im Jahr 2023 hat sich die Green Belt GmbH als Vorreiter für nachhaltiges Batteriemanagement etabliert. Mit innovativen Lösungen setzen wir neue Maßstäbe und unterstützen unsere Kunden bei der Verwaltung und Überwachung von Batteriethemen:

Die komplette Lösung für Batterieverwaltung und -sicherheit!

CO₂-neutrale Server als Grundlage für nachhaltiges und effizientes Umweltmanagement.

Unser Anspruch reicht weit über die Einhaltung gesetzlicher Vorgaben hinaus. Wir optimieren kontinuierlich unsere Prozesse, um Umwelt- und Klimaschutz aktiv voranzutreiben. Dabei steht die Schonung von Ressourcen und die Förderung nachhaltiger Lösungen stets im Fokus.

Gemeinsam gestalten wir die Zukunft – innovativ, nachhaltig und verantwortungsbewusst. Für eine Welt, die auch kommenden Generationen lebenswert bleibt.

www.green-belt.eu

Impressum  https://www.green-belt.eu/impressum 

Datenschutz  https://www.green-belt.eu/datenschutz

Name: Tany Ketsa
Description: Tany Ketsa is a women- led youth organization in Madagascar dedicated to the environmental education of primary school children.

Name: Plasticraft Ltd
Description:
Plasticraft Ltd is a pioneering social enterprise in Rwanda dedicated to advancing circular economy through sustainable recycling and creative craftsmanship. We specialize in collecting plastic waste from local communities in Kigali and its suburbs, repurposing it into functional crafts that showcases Rwanda's cultural heritage.

We aim to close the plastic pollution loop and empower women within the waste value chain, turning environmental challenges into sustainable livelihoods through innovative solutions.

At the heart of our mission is Kwanda Green; a vocational training program focused on empowering underserved women and youth with hands on green skills, preparing them for entry into Rwanda's emerging green jobs market. Our goal is to ensure these skills become stable sources of income, while collectively accelerating Rwanda's ambition to become a carbon-neutral economy by 2050.

Join us in crafting a Greener Future, where waste transforms into value, and communities thrive through sustainable practices.

Name: The Green Point Initiative
Description:
The Green Point Initiative (GPI) strives to raise awareness of the importance of going green and advocate for climate action through engaging in human-centered design training and boosting what we call social 'agripreneurship' mindsets in young people. 

GPI engages in environmental activities that encourage youth participation such as planting trees and community clean-ups.

GPI also aims to co-develop innovative agribusinesses with young people and build sustainable income through the green economy for ambassadors who participate in the initiative.

Name: River Action
Description:
We work to protect and restore the Mississippi River and our shared RiverWay environment.

We strive to foster cultural and economic activities that showcase the Mississippi River and our shared RiverWay environment.

We promote awareness and adoption of sustainable practices that enhance the Mississippi River and our shared RiverWay environment.

We achieve our vision by:
Collaborating and partnering with people who appreciate and understand the importance of the Mississippi River and its place within the Quad Cities region.

Advocating for the protection and enjoyment of the Mississippi River for people within the Quad Cities region.

Developing and presenting programs, activities and projects that positively impact the people of the Quad Cities region and provide opportunities to enjoy the Mississippi River.

Educating people in the Quad Cities region about the history, environmental impact and uniqueness of the Mississippi River.

Respecting the natural beauty and magnificence of the Mississippi River and recognizing those people who share this respect within the Quad Cities region.

Name: Global Rainforest Support Organization
Description: Supporting our world's disappearing rainforests

Name: Sen pollution
Description: Bienvenue chez SEN POLLUTION, une initiative passionnée dédiée à la préservation des précieuses ressources en eau du Sénégal. Nous utilisons des technologies de pointe, notamment des données satellitaires avancées, pour mesurer et évaluer le degré de pollution de nos cours d'eau, qu'il s'agisse de majestueux fleuves, de lacs paisibles, d'estuaires essentiels ou de zones humides cruciales.

Name: We ReUse
Description:
Woman and Veteran founded; We ReUse is a sustainability company focused on eliminating single-use foodware with recycled stainless steel to reduce gas emissions, save water, and minimize waste at landfills. We ReUse consults with schools and businesses to develop a reuse plan that focuses on reducing single-use by healthier, more durable solutions that positively impact the school and the planet.
The comprehensive reuse plan will include options for reusable products that are made in the U.S. as well as suggestions for internal logistics; ultimately reducing water, energy, and landfill waste.

Name: Saldanha Bay Clean Air Association
Description:
Welcome to the Saldanha Bay Clean Air Association (SBCAA). Founded in 2018, we are a non-profit organization committed to monitoring, analyzing, and reporting on air quality data within the Saldanha Bay Municipal area.

Our work is rooted in scientific research and evidence-based methods to ensure transparency and accuracy. By capturing real-time data and compiling annual "State of the Atmosphere" reports, we provide the community, industries, and policymakers with the information they need to make informed decisions

Name: Bénédicte Beliaeff - Qualité & Environnement
Description:
Assistance à Maîtrise d'Ouvrage: Pour que vos projets de rénovation ou de construction se montrent sereins, valorisants et respectueux.
Je vous accompagne et vous conseille pour respecter budget et délais, atteindre les performances énergétiques ou environnementales fixées, obtenir un label valorisant.

Name: NW Compliance Consulting
Description:
NW Compliance Consulting assists companies and small businesses with compliance-related documentation and trainings, for Food Safety, Stormwater and Wastewater compliance.  Founder Anne Ivans is an experienced quality manager in Food Production, Stormwater and Wastewater.  Ivans creates systems that were sound, scientific, and effective. 
Food Safety documentation support examples: HACCP and GMP Training Programs, Quality Complaint Resolution Systems, Audit Corrective Actions, and Validation and Shelf-life Reports.  
Stormwater support: Quarterly Reports, Tier I and Tier II written for you and submitted to the DEQ, Company-specific Training Programs, and record-keeping review and audit-readiness assistance.  
Wastewater support examples: Monitoring Forms, Training Documents, Chemical Handling Procedures and Trainings, Spill Prevention and Response Documentation.  
NWCC will work with you, as an outside contractor, evaluating your current compliance plan(s) and your current risks.  The goal is that your processes are consistent with best practices, your workers are trained and motivated to work within procedures that align with actual work practices and compliance regulations, and you are audit ready 24/7.
When trainings are meaningful, top performers are motivated to sustain audit-readiness programs.  NWCC moves companies out of the reactive mode and into the culture changing continuous improvement mode.

Name: River Science
Description:
River Science (formerly, Uviation World Water) was organized to mobilize people, funding, and technology to achieve the greatest water conservation impacts, improving water security, ecosystems, and the ocean. River Science collaborates with local schools, universities, researchers, government, businesses, and communities to understand, identify, and remediate water conservation issues. As a non-profit, River Science can help initiate projects that would otherwise not be commercially viable. Through these actions, River Science pursues scientific research for public interest, and makes all generated research freely available to students, the public, and scientific publications.

River Science believes in open, affordable, and globally scalable technology and programs, facilitating a cultural shift to restore and replenish our natural resources.

Name: CyberCharge LLC
Description:
Introducing CyberCharge: Revolutionizing EV Charging! 

Founded in 2024, CyberCharge is a startup on a mission to transform the electric vehicle (EV) charging landscape with our patent-pending, on-demand mobile EV2EV charging solutions.

Proposed Services:

Mobile EV Charging: Efficient charging at roadside, beaches, parks, and businesses.

Emergency Charging: Quick response for EV owners with depleted batteries.

Subscription Services: Flexible plans for regular EV users.

Technology Vision: We leverage cutting-edge technology for fast, efficient charging. Our mobile units will feature advanced systems and smart grid integration for optimized energy usage.

Market Opportunity: With the EV market expanding rapidly, CyberCharge aims to fill the gap in convenient and accessible charging infrastructure, ensuring uninterrupted mobility for EV owners.

Our Team:

Jeff Johnson, Co-founder and CEO: Leading with extensive experience in engineering and technology.

Jacob Goralski, PE, Partner and Licensed Professional Engineer: Ensuring our services meet the highest standards.

Strategic Partnerships: We plan to collaborate with leading tech firms and energy providers to enhance our offerings and benefit from the latest advancements.

Sustainability Commitment: Our mobile units will optimize energy usage and reduce environmental impact, supporting the transition to clean energy.

Future Plans: CyberCharge aims to expand our service area and technology to meet growing demand, scaling operations nationwide and exploring new markets.

For more information about CyberCharge, visit CyberCharge.us or contact us at admin@cybercharge.us.

#EVCharging #ElectricVehicles #Sustainability #Innovation #CleanEnergy #Startup #TechInnovation #CyberCharge #PatentPending

Name: GREEEN YOUTH ALIANCE
Description:
Green Youth Alliance is a dynamic organization committed to empowering young people to lead the charge in environmental sustainability and climate action. Through education, advocacy, and hands-on initiatives, we engage youth to address pressing environmental challenges and drive positive change in their communities. Our mission is to inspire and equip the next generation of environmental leaders by providing them with the knowledge, skills, and opportunities to create a greener, more sustainable world. Join us as we work together to protect the planet and secure a healthier future for all.

For inquiries, contact us at:  

greenyouthaliance@gmail.com

Name: Green Stewardship Hub
Description:
Sustainable Environmental Practices Advocacy Hub is a platform dedicated to promoting sustainable living through education, advocacy, and community engagement. We empower individuals and organizations to take climate-conscious action via workshops, digital outreach, and awareness campaigns.

Guided by SDG 13, we’re envisioning a future where innovative solutions like a Circular Economy Marketplace, an AI-powered waste-matching system, and a Carbon Footprint Tracker is made available for local communities—making sustainability both accessible and rewarding.

 Motto: Advocacy for Earth and Action for ALL.

Name: EcOsolucionar
Description: Soluciones para la reducción de costos en ambientes residenciales, empresariales e instituciones educativas públicas y privadas.

Name: FRESSCO UK
Description:
Fressco was a environmentally friendly project that took place from 2019 to 2023 sponsored by the UK government and the University of Glasgow Caledonian. The project started with the noble mission of promoting sustainable consumption and developing strategies to mitigate carbon emissions resulting from the production and transportation of Fast-Moving Consumer Goods (FMCG). As a digital aggregator, Fressco has implemented a technology-driven solution that empowers customers to actively participate in environmental conservation.

Key Achievements:
 Supporting Local, Sustainable Products: We exclusively support local, plastic-free, and meat-free products across England, reinforcing our commitment to sustainability.
 Nationwide Expansion: Initially serving the London area, we expanded our delivery services nationwide within just five months, reflecting rapid growth and customer demand in England. 
 Carbon Footprint Reduction Initiatives: Through partnerships with organizations such as Saving The Amazon and Team Trees, we have planted thousands of trees in the Amazon rainforest, actively contributing to carbon offsetting efforts.
 Food reduction initiatives:  Through our partnership with Karma we donate surplus food in order to minimize waste and support our community, actively contributing to environmental sustainability and social responsibility.
 Award Recognition: In the summer of 2021, we were honored with the Great British Entrepreneur Award in the ‘Entrepreneur for Good’ category, acknowledging our dedication to environmental sustainability and ethical business practices.  ￼

Our customers value our environmentally conscious approach, and our suppliers share our commitment to sustainable practices.

Name: Equalitas certification
Description:
Equalitas has implemented a documented management system, meeting the requirements of various standard for providing 3rd party management system certification services.
Equalitas maintains customer focus, service credibility and provide independent, impartial, dynamic, value added and cost effective management system certification services.
Equalitas understands the importance of impartiailty in carrying out its management system certification activities, manages conflict of interest and ensures the objectivity of its management system certification activities.

Name: The Southwood Foundation
Description: TSF is a charity working on the nature crisis and climate change, in particular in London and the South-East.  Our mission is to speed up action and foster collaboration so people and nature can thrive together.  We help by advocating for and connecting action, convening and building community, facilitating joined up thinking and engaging with groups not traditionally involved in nature recovery. We are a partner in the 100 mile Weald to Waves nature corridor.

Name: DeCoWaste SUDOE
Description: DeCoWaste busca la gestión sostenible de los residuos de construcción y demolición de las PYMES del SUDOE, alineada con la Directiva Marco de Residuos de la UE, mediante mejor separación y valorización para para reducir contaminación y optimizar recursos.

Name: IUCN Green List
Description:
Originally launched as a certification programme, the IUCN Green List for Protected and Conserved Areas has evolved into a global benchmark for enhancing equitable and effective management and governance of protected and conserved areas (PCAs). By 2030, the Green List aims to firmly establish its standard as a key framework for advancing biodiversity conservation under the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework (GBF), with a particular focus on supporting Target 3 - Conserve 30% of Land, Waters and Seas . It will also continue to align with other major environmental conventions and programmes, such as World Heritage and Ramsar, to amplify its impact. 

The institutional governance of the IUCN Green List Programme ensures efficient implementation combined with robust and impartial decisions based on expert judgement and verification that are locally relevant.

Our mission is to foster a global impact by promoting environmental stewardship and social responsibility. We believe in the power of collective action and strive to bridge the gap between the educated and uneducated.

Join us as we lead initiatives that improve environmental quality, support sustainable agriculture, and educate communities on the importance of preserving our planet. Together, we can make a difference and build a brighter, greener future for all.

Name: Arabian Quality Masters
Description: Arabian Quality Masters (AQM) is an organization that specializes in Quality Systems certification and training related to the ISO (International Organization for Standardization) standards. AQM provides a range of services to help organizations improve their quality management systems, including ISO certification, consulting, training, and auditing. Their focus is on helping businesses achieve excellence in quality management, and they have a team of experienced professionals who provide customized solutions tailored to meet the specific needs of their clients. AQM aims to promote quality management practices in the Arab region and beyond, and they work with a wide range of industries to ensure compliance with international quality standards.
