We spread awareness about climate change and what we can do about it.
We also carry out training activities and talks for schools, companies and government departments on global warming, ecological habits and what is our role as human beings in this context of climate crisis.
Our actual project is "Ecobotellas". Wich consist in recycling single-use plastics or disposable plastics. These items are things like plastic bags, straws, coffee stirrers, soda and water bottles and most food packaging. We close the cycle of this material by converting it into recycled plastic lumber(RPL).

Name: Bee Pimpn
Description:
Our company is proud to offer a range of products and services centered around the wonderful world of bees. With a dedication to sustainability, education, and professional management, we aim to foster a deeper appreciation for these incredible insects and their invaluable contributions to our environment. Our products include high-quality honey, sourced from the finest bee colonies to ensure the purest taste, and versatile beeswax, meticulously processed for crafting candles, skincare products, and more.
We also provide a variety of services such as educational programs designed for all ages and skill levels, migratory pollination services to enhance crop yields and promote biodiversity, professional hive management to help companies maintain healthy colonies on their campus, and honey bee removal conducted with the utmost care and respect for the property and the bees. In summary, our company is dedicated to supporting bee health and promoting sustainable practices through our products and services, making a positive impact on both our customers and the natural world.

Name: Eco Revive
Description: Eco Revive is dedicated to igniting change and creating a sustainable future. Driven by a passion for protecting our planet, we champion eco-friendly practices, inspire bold action, and lead transformative initiatives to conserve nature and combat climate change. Together, we build a greener, better world.

Name: ACN Goes Green
Description:
FACEM LUMEA UN LOC MAI BUN!

Suntem oameni! Întâmpinăm greutățile prin care trece mediul înconjurător și înfruntăm nepăsarea. Pădurile sunt ținuturi fantastice, cu multiple rolului în mediu; o inspirație pentru cele mai îndrăznețe basme. Și ce-ar fi basmele fără de monștrii xilofagi înfometați de averi, nu? Refuzăm să fim spectatori la filmul distrugerilor, într-un scenariu de prost gust. E greu să lupți cu capetele monstruoase, desfigurate de intenții sinistre, e greu să fii „ăla bun” din poveste, dar dacă nu acum, atunci când?

Așa că ne-am unit forțele. Vrem să construim lucruri pe care nu le poți avea de unul singur, ci numai cu apărarea unor oameni de încredere, pe bratele cărora să te lași atunci când simți că nu mai poți.  

    Echipa noastră este unică! Împreună vom reface poezia naturii: avem nevoie de pomi, ca de cuvinte! Vrem să-i plantăm, așa cum le rostim, oferind pământului ceea ce i se cuvine: stâlpi solizi, columnatici, în scoarța cărora să rămână sudoarea muncii noastre, căci ne-am străduit să ne salvăm Planeta.

Name: Milieu Academy
Description:
Milieu Academy is dé partner voor scholen die hun leerlingen willen voorbereiden op een betekenisvolle carrière in het milieuvak. 

We maken het makkelijk en aantrekkelijk voor scholieren, studenten en mensen die een nieuwe richting op willen in hun werk, om te kiezen voor een loopbaan in de milieusector.

Onze missie is: een nieuwe generatie deskundigen op te leiden die niet alleen voldoen aan de strenge certificeringseisen van ons vakgebied, maar die ook een diepe verbinding hebben met hun lokale omgeving. 

Met andere woorden: er staat ons iets te doen!

Dus ben jij een school of praktijkopleider en wil jij het milieuvak promoten bij jouw leerlingen? Neem een kijkje op milieuacademy.nl voor meer informatie en voor het aanvragen van ons lesprogramma.

Name: Grupo Rutas
Description:
Fundada en 1990, Rutas nació con el propósito de ofrecer soluciones integrales para la gestión forestal, agrícola y cinegética, impulsada por una profunda pasión por la naturaleza. Con más de tres décadas de experiencia, nos especializamos en la gestión sostenible de fincas rústicas, manteniendo un equilibrio saludable entre los ecosistemas y las actividades humanas.

En Rutas, entendemos la caza como una herramienta de conservación, donde el objetivo principal no es la captura, sino contribuir a la mejora del ecosistema. Nuestras prácticas responsables ayudan a controlar las poblaciones animales, preservar la salud de la fauna y flora, y mantener el equilibrio entre las especies cinegéticas y no cinegéticas. A través de una gestión adecuada, no solo promovemos la sostenibilidad, sino también la salud de la cubierta vegetal, evitando problemas como la erosión y la pérdida de biodiversidad.

Ofrecemos una amplia gama de servicios que van desde la planificación y organización de actividades cinegéticas hasta la implementación de infraestructuras y trabajos forestales y agrícolas. Nuestro enfoque holístico garantiza que cada decisión que tomamos está alineada con el equilibrio natural y la sostenibilidad a largo plazo.

En Rutas, estamos comprometidos en proporcionar soluciones personalizadas que respondan a las necesidades específicas de nuestros clientes, siempre con el objetivo de generar un impacto positivo en el medio ambiente.

Name: My Blue Journey

Our approach is to follow a lifecycle road map to help communities understand and work with the ocean and sea. Through Blue Tech and Blue Economy initiatives we hope to create communities that not only enjoy the ocean, but are fed by it and will protect it through education and vocational skills.

We equip students and teachers with the knowledge and tools to drive meaningful change through innovative, hands-on programs like project-based learning, real-time environmental monitoring, and advanced technologies (AR, VR, and QR).

Partnering with schools and communities, we provide practical workshops, EcoLabs, and an interactive Earth Pulse Hub for real-world learning in air and water quality, soil health, and sustainable agriculture. Our gamified EcoPioneer Badge System motivates students as they tackle environmental challenges while teachers receive support and training to foster impactful project-based learning.

Together, we’re building a generation ready to lead a sustainable future. Join us on our journey to inspire and equip the next generation of environmental champions.

Earth Champions Academy is made possible through the Meridian-Funded Opportunity: SUSI Mini-Grants, with partial funding provided by the U.S. Department of State’s Bureau of Educational and Cultural Affairs via Meridian International Center as the implementing partner.

Name: Polis Litoral Ria de Aveiro – Sociedade para a Requalificação e  Valorização da Ria de Aveiro, S.A.
Description:
A Sociedade tem como objecto a gestão, a coordenação e a execução do investimento a realizar no âmbito do Polis Litoral Ria de Aveiro — Operação Integrada de Requalificação e Valorização da Ria de Aveiro, na área e nos termos definidos no respectivo plano estratégico, compreendendo igualmente o desenvolvimento das acções estruturantes previstas naquele documento em matéria de valorização e requalificação ambiental e urbana, de dinamização de actividades turísticas, culturais, de lazer e de outras intervenções que contribuam para o desenvolvimento económico e social da sua área de intervenção.

A Sociedade tem ainda por missão a realização de projectos e acções que conduzam ao desenvolvimento associado à preservação do património natural e paisagístico, que inclui acções de protecção e requalificação da zona costeira visando a prevenção de risco, a promoção da conservação da natureza e biodiversidade no âmbito de uma gestão sustentável, a valorização de actividades tradicionais ligadas aos recursos da ria de Aveiro, a requalificação e revitalização das frentes lagunares, a valorização dos núcleos piscatórios marítimos e lagunares e a qualificação e ordenamento da mobilidade na ria de Aveiro, a valorização dos «espaços ria» para fruição pública e a promoção do património natural e cultural a ela associado.

Name: Latvijas Vides pārvaldības asociācija (LVPA)
Description:
LVPA ir biedrība, kas ir dibināta 1998. gadā ar mērķi apvienot vides pārvaldības speciālistus, uzņēmumus, kā arī sabiedrības pārstāvjus Latvijas ilgtspējīgas attīstības veicināšanai, ieviešot tīrākas ražošanas, vides pārvaldības un ilgtspējīgas attīstības principus Latvijas uzņēmumos un pašvaldībās. 

LVPA darbības virzieni cita starpā iekļauj:
  •	Zināšanu par tīrākajām tehnoloģijām un videi draudzīgiem darbības veidiem uzkrāšanu un popularizēšanu 
•	Informācijas izplatīšanu par vides pārvaldības sistēmām un to ieviešanas metodēm
•	Sadarbības projektu īstenošanu (gan ar vietējo, gan ārvalstu partneru līdzdalību) tīrāko tehnoloģiju, vides pārvaldības sistēmu un ilgtspējīga patēriņa principu ieviešanā
•	Sadarbības projektu ar valsts pārvaldes institūcijām īstenošanu ar mērķi pilnveidot normatīvo regulējumu un vides pārvaldības sistēmas gan valsts, gan uzņēmumu līmenī
•	Informatīvo un pieredzes apmaiņas pasākumu organizēšanu uzņēmumiem, pašvaldībām un citām ieinteresētajām pusēm
•	Līdzdarbību gan Latvijas, gan Eiropas Savienības likumdošanas aktu izstrādē, nodrošinot uzņēmumu interešu pārstāvniecību.

Name: Great Basin Unified Air Pollution Control District
Description:
Inyo, Mono and Alpine Counties joined together in 1974 in a joint powers agreement to form the Great Basin Unified Air Pollution Control District, which covers the Great Basin Valleys Air Basin in California.

GBUAPCD's purpose is to enforce federal, state and local air quality regulations and to ensure that the federal and state air quality standards are met in the District. These standards are set to protect the health of sensitive individuals by restricting how much pollution is allowed in the air.

To meet these standards GBUAPCD enforces those federal laws delegated to us, state laws on stationary sources of pollution, and pass and enforce our own regulations as they become necessary.

Name: Bombay Environmental Action Group
Description:
The Bombay Environmental Action Group (BEAG) is an environmental NGO founded in 1979 by a group of professionals, artists, nature enthusiasts and entrepreneurs in Mumbai who were concerned about the impact of industrial and residential development on the environment and on local populations.

In 1974, a group of farmers and fishermen in Alibag approached civil right activists living in the area for help. They had heard that their lands were going to be forcibly acquired by the government for a chemical factory.

The activists were able to confirm from official sources that the government was in fact planning to acquire land between Alibag town and Mandwa village to build a large petrochemical complex, However, no more information was available to the activists or to the local population.

What is COLA?
The Coalition of Lake Associations is united for the purpose of:

Sharing their experience in lake water and community association management
 
Increasing their knowledge of lake association problems and alternative solutions

Aeration

Description:

The Dales Land Net Soil Monitor provides a view of what is going on in the soil layers below the surface.
• Real time data using IoT networks
• 50cm depth
• 4 moisture zones
• 6 temperature depths
• Self charging
• No need for wires or for data loggers.
• Far more affordable than similar spec models on the market.

Food Safety Management Systems: Design, implementation, and certification support for HACCP, ISO 22000, and FSSC 22000 standards across hotels, hospitals, and catering operations.

Healthcare Quality and Sustainability: Green hospital transformation programs, waste management optimization, carbon footprint assessment, and accreditation preparation (GAHAR, JCI, GGHH).

Training and Capacity Building: Development of tailored training programs for food handlers, hospital staff, and quality officers on hygiene, infection control, and environmental health.

Auditing and Compliance: Independent internal and external audits to ensure adherence to local and international food and health safety regulations.

Advisory Services: Technical support for sustainability initiatives, energy and water efficiency, and climate-smart healthcare operations.

EQuality Assured’s mission is to bridge quality with sustainability, promoting healthier communities through improved food safety, environmental responsibility, and green healthcare transformation.

Name: The Liberian Geographical Society - LGS
Description:
The Liberian Geographical Society (LGS) is a non-profit academic body of academics, practitioners, and educators in nationwide geography, GIS, and environmental issues. It was established in 2017 by a team of Liberian academics, professionals, and teachers under the auspices of the IAYG, an international organization in geographic education. 

As part of our activities, we organize events for geography, GIS, and environmental studies educators, connecting educators and linking them with valuable educational materials. We also organize events for students in high schools and create awareness of the need to protect our environment; we work with community leaders and municipal authorities to spread the message of keeping our environment clean. We hope to put development on the map, helping advance this critical field of study, which develops spatial reasoning skills necessary for knowledge economy careers and helps chart Liberia’s global future.  

LGS's mission is to help develop the next generation of geographers and environmental professionals whose innovations will help to address the major unmet needs of our global community. Our focus objective is to promote geographical and environmental education across Liberia through research, training, mentorship, and coaching. 

LGS also has over four years of rich experience in providing technical geospatial services in the use of satellite imagery for participatory land use planning, urban land use planning, natural resources assessment, customized GIS applications Development, GPS data processing, land surveying, area mapping and zoning,  capacity building, and map production.  LGS specializes in providing solutions for multi-sectoral geospatial projects, including area surveying, zoning, and mapping of infrastructure in both urban and rural areas. We have executed several assignments related to forest monitoring, urban land inventory, developing ICT architecture, and tool development for monitoring purposes.

Name: Re-Cycling - Bike reuse and riding fairs
Description:
The overall objective of the “Re-Cycling” project is to re-establish a healthy life-style among kids, and, at the same time, to promote social inclusion and a circular economy in the biking sector. Tackling the dropping-out from sport, caused by COVID emergency, and re-establishing community bonds especially for those who paid the highest price of isolation – the children - is a common need of the partners involved. In line with the call goal “addressing common needs”, the partnership will deal with this urgency by promoting biking as a heathy, affordable outdoor activity. Moreover, the project will contribute to the call objective “enabling transformation” in partner organizations since “Re-cycling” is a citizen-led innovation which combines sport, green mobility and consumers’ empowerment through the “Green & Sustainable biking” training program and the “Re-Cycling” Fairs. the project will address the following priorities of E+ programme:
a) Inclusion and diversity. The project will promote the participation of kids in sport, which has decreased due to the Covid-19 restrictions, thus fuelling health problems among the youngsters. Children and parents from low SES families will be the main target group as they have been impacted more by the pandemic, in terms of socio-economic negative effects. The circular economy approach (repairing and reusing bikes) will also make biking more affordable for those families who are at present experiencing financial hardship. Bike rides will also strengthen community ties against isolation.  
b) environment and fight against climate change. Biking is green mobility per se. By encouraging family to use bikes as a means of transport the project will contribute to this Program priority. A specific training program on “Green & Sustainable Biking” and the Re-cycling Fair will leverage this environmental impact.

Name: Environment & Power Systems International, LLC
Description:
Environment & Power Systems International, LLC was organized in Illinois in 2003, to develop and commercialize a  cogeneration  technology with integrated air pollution controls designed to abate criteria air pollutants subject to the National Ambient Air Quality Standards (NAAQS) offering states cost-effective solutions for environmental implementation planning and to improve competitiveness and growth of stationary manufacturing industries 
A registered foreign company in Arizona, the Company is headquartered in the high-tech center of Oro Valley, Arizona. The Company is associated with seasoned business partners and suppliers that have the skills, knowledge and expertise to manufacture small-scale, yet scalable systems for turn-key projects from conception and engineering to aftermarket operations and maintenance. 
 
VALUE PROPOSITION
Our energy and environmental solution features Vocgen gas turbine oxidizer (GTO) including tactical auxiliary equipment in support of a superior energy savings model for over 100 discrete industrial categories subject to the requirements of Title V Air Operating Permits (CAA); significantly reducing the life-cycle cost of traditional volatile organic compound (VOC) air pollution control equipment; while providing the reliability and economic benefits of cogeneration (CHP) while offsetting central power plant emissions pursuant to the US EPA Clean Power Plan and Guidelines 
Our economic model significantly reduces operational costs by burning waste volatile organics in mixtures of standard liquid and gaseous fuels with optional biofuels (ethanol) cogenerating clean power and high-quality waste heat. Operational savings benefits the Client (end-user) by providing a return on capital investment (approximately 2 years) from positive annual cash flows for the life of the project estimated at 20-30 years. Products and projects are sold at competitive air pollution control market-based prices.

Name: See Through Carbon
Description:
An innovative accurate, transparent, open-source, free to use carbon reporting ecosystem, designed to help us treat greenhouse gas emissions with the same degree of seriousness, rigour and detail as financial reporting.

STC enables big companies to comply with increasingly stringent carbon reporting regulations by facilitating accurate Scope 3 reporting for both them and their supply chains.

STC's model combines various proven ecosystem features, such as:
* Linux's underlying technical architecture
* Wikipedia's outsourced data creation and quality control
* The UN's Sustainable Development Goals systematic framing

to create a public database to both facilitate regulatory compliance, and provide data to analyse and train AI models to help measurably reduce carbon.

Being free to use, and deploying creative storytelling techniques unfamiliar to most tech-based consultancies, STC is tackling carbon reporting's greatest challenge - how to convince SMEs, for whom carbon reporting in not yet mandatory and who can't afford commercial  standards, to accurately report their carbon footprints at scale, so that their bigger customers, for whom accurate Scope 3 reporting is increasing a legal requirement, to comply. 

Current carbon auditing standards neglect small businesses because they lack the resources to pay for carbon auditing and consultancy, but they emit twice as much carbon as the bigger businesses at the top of the supply chain.

See Through Carbon is created and developed by a global network of pro bono experts without external funding, and is supported via ESG and CSR programmes.  It will always be free to use.

If you don't pay for a standard, you can't influence it.

If you can't buy integrity, why should you be able to sell it?

Name: Luppitt Landscape Partnership
Description:
The Luppitt Landscape Partnership (LLP) is one of 34 projects across England selected by Defra for development funding during Round 2 of Landscape Recovery.  

The LLP is taking a holistic approach to nurturing a resilient, self-reliant, ecologically-connected valley around the village of Luppitt, covering a 20 sq km sub-catchment of the River Otter in East Devon.  The aim is to conserve and extend wildlife habitats, improve ecosystem function, address net zero objectives, and provide opportunities for education, training and community participation.  These aims are being pursued in order to create better conditions for economically and environmentally sustainable farm businesses to continue and thrive.  

The LLP is a new, landowner-led Community Interest Company with directors drawn from its farming and landowning membership, which comprises 55 holdings covering 1980 hectares in the Luppitt valley, including commercial farmers, non-farming larger landowners and smallholders.  

The project development period began in spring 2024 and will run for 2 years.  The LLP employs a project manager and administrator and a small team of specialists, funded through a development grant from Defra.

Name: Back to Blue
Description:
Back to Blue is a national movement to heal our waters. Our mission: restore the health of freshwater ecosystems by protecting what’s left and repairing what’s been harmed.

It began in Tampa. Founder Michael Beck watched his local rivers choke with trash and his neighbors lose hope. Instead of turning away, his community grabbed bags and buckets and went into action. Together, they pulled garbage from rivers, demanded stronger protections, and tried to hold the line. But the tide kept turning. Red tide blooms grew worse. Trash piled up faster than it could be cleared. Manatees began starving to death. The crisis had outgrown any single town.

“The truth is, not everyone in this country has an ocean to care about,” Beck says. “But everyone has a lake or a river that matters to them. I may not be able to change the world, but I can take care of my corner of it and hopefully spark others to do the same. If each of us takes responsibility for our piece of the puzzle, together we can build something enduring, something that will protect our waters for generations to come.”

That vision stretches beyond cleanups. Beck imagines a future where waterways are safeguarded by smart technologies and automated systems. Solutions like bubble barriers that intercept plastic before it reaches open water, filtration booms that trap pollutants, and other innovations that can keep rivers pristine without constant human labor. Back to Blue champions these tools while also fostering collaboration to invent new ones, uniting scientists, eco-tech startups, and national brands to scale solutions across the country.

Water is life. Clean water is a right. And now more than ever, we must act.
Join us and our partners across the nation as we go Back to Blue, together.

Name: Blue Project
Description:
O Blue Project pretende construir uma linha estratégica de ação, que tem como ponto de partida a promoção do consumo do pescado local, assegurar as quotas de captura, promover o reaproveitamento excedente da costa portuguesa e reduzir o desperdício alimentar. O sarrajão (Katsuwonus pelamis) é uma das espécies que necessita de ser mais conhecida e consumida pela população. Este peixe existe em abundância, tem um sabor aproximado ao do atum e, por comparação, é economicamente mais viável.

Processo
Através de uma colaboração estreita com parceiros do setor da Investigação e Desenvolvimento (I&D), pretende-se atingir dois objetivos específicos: entregar um produto saboroso, saudável e de elevado valor nutricional às escolas, promovendo o consumo de filetes de peixe pelos alunos; e potenciar o aproveitamento de todas as sobras do peixe após a filetagem do pescado excedente.

Resultados Esperados
Espera-se que o Blue Project consiga promover a mudança de atitudes e de comportamentos face aos atuais problemas globais, educando os consumidores para uma escolha consciente, informada e sustentável. Através da valorização da atividade piscatória local, pretende-se envolver a comunidade científica, a indústria e a sociedade num projeto audaz e altamente necessário.

Esta iniciativa é financiada pelo fundo EEA Grants Portugal.

Name: CENTRE FOR ENVIRONMENT, HUMAN RIGHTS AND DEVELOPMENT
Description:

We also monitor, document, and report human rights violations across the region. In some cases, CEHRD provides legal advocacy to victims of human rights violations.
With an unemployment rate of over 70%, dire poverty, and dim prospects, some young men seek financial and physical security by joining armed gangs, or "cults".  Local government officials and/or community leaders carry out campaigns of violence designed to influence local decision-making, especially during elections, and periodically sponsor many of these cult groups. Often, these gangs are also used to sway village leaders’ and communities' opinions on oil-related projects.  In all cases, the influx of cheap arms has led to a drastic increase in violence.  CEHRD monitors the trend and campaigns against it. 

VISION
A just society where fundamental human rights are attained.

MISSION
To galvanize stakeholders to protect human rights and achieve sustainable development through inclusive partnerships.

www.cehrd.org.ng

Name: FRAM
Description:
Project FRAM (Fire Risk Assessment and Management of European Coal Heaps) addresses one of Europe’s most urgent post-mining environmental challenges – the spontaneous combustion of coal spoil heaps. These sites release toxic gases, greenhouse emissions, threatening both ecosystems and local communities. 

FRAM introduces a pioneering solution by combining thermal imaging, real-time gas sensors, and predictive models with renewable-energy-powered monitoring stations. 

A key project outcome is a European-wide inventory of coal spoil heaps, identifying high-risk sites and providing crucial tools for reclamation strategies and policy development. 

FRAM contributes to EU climate goals and supports the Just Transition by transforming coal legacies into safer, more sustainable environments.

Name: Virginia Association of Soil and Water Conservation Districts
Description:
The Virginia Association of Soil and Water Conservation Districts (VASWCD) is a private nonprofit association of 47 soil and water conservation districts in Virginia and is classified accordingly as a 501(c)(5). It is a voluntary, nongovernmental association of Virginia’s districts.

The VASWCD’s voting members are all elected and appointed directors. SWCDs were established in the 1930’s as political subdivisions of the state in response to the pressing need for the conservation of our natural resources in the Commonwealth. Each local SWCD is directed by a board of elected and appointed directors who are elected in general elections and serve four year terms. The VASWCD is governed by a 12-member board, all of whom must also be Directors. A President, 1st Vice President, Past President, Secretary/Treasurer and representative to the National Association of Conservation Districts (NACD) constitute the executive committee and are empowered to act on behalf of the board between meetings.

The state of Virginia is divided into six geographic areas. SWCD directors within each area annually elect two directors to serve as Area Chair and Area Vice Chair. The Area Chair serves on the VASWCD Board and represents his/her respective SWCDs.

MISSION
The mission of the Virginia Association of Soil and Water Conservation Districts and its Foundation is to provide and promote leadership in the conservation of natural resources through stewardship and education programs. The VASWCD and its Foundation coordinates conservation efforts statewide to focus effectively on issues identified by local member districts.

VISION
The VASWCD will be an effective, independent voice in Virginia’s conservation partnership supporting the missions of Soil and Water Conservation Districts by providing leadership and training, improving communication, promoting financial independence, and providing cohesive representation to legislative bodies on natural resource issues.

Name: SPM LAB SOLUTIONS
Description:
SPM LAB SOLUTIONS is your complete partner in calibration, validation, water quality testing, and Certified Reference Materials (CRM). We specialize in the calibration of APC, APM, and other critical laboratory instruments, ensuring the highest standards of accuracy and reliability.

Our services extend to cleanroom validation, precision testing for Total Organic Carbon (TOC), conductivity, and ozone levels in water. As an ISO 9001:2015 certified and ISO/IEC 17025:2017 accredited laboratory, as well as ISO 17034 accredited for Certified Reference Materials (CRM), we provide industry-leading expertise in laboratory services and support.

In addition to calibration and testing, we offer sales, service, and maintenance for scientific and environmental equipment. Whether it's design, installation, or ongoing compliance support, SPM LAB SOLUTIONS provides comprehensive solutions tailored to meet the needs of industries that demand precision and reliability.

With a focus on quality, compliance, and customer satisfaction, we are committed to delivering trusted solutions that help you maintain the highest standards in your operations.

La qualità del servizio si fonda su tecniche di rilevamento che sono divenute via via sempre più precise, grazie alla dotazione di strumentazione altamente competitive che vengono periodicamente rinnovate secondo un piano di investimenti quinquennale. La rete opera in interconnessione con altre centraline gestite da Arpa e LCC; il numero considerevole di stazioni presenti nel territorio e il continuo scambio/confronto di dati e misure testimonia una significativa qualità nel servizio complessivo di monitoraggio dell'area.

Name: ENVIROP
Description:
O nás – ENVIROP: Výzkum pro ochranu přírody a krajiny 

Pracovní skupina ENVIROP (Environmental Management and Nature Conservation Research Group) se jako součást Ústavu botaniky a zoologie při Přírodovědecké fakultě Masarykovy univerzity věnuje výzkumu zaměřenému na ochranu biodiverzity, ekologickou obnovu a udržitelný management přírodních stanovišť. Naše aktivity zahrnují jak teoretický výzkum, tak aplikované projekty přispívající k obnově a dlouhodobému udržení přírodních ekosystémů.

Na čem pracujeme:
 Ekologická obnova a revitalizace krajiny – řešíme projekty spojené s obnovou mokřadů, luk, stepních oblastí a slanisek.
 Ochrana biodiverzity – sledujeme druhovou rozmanitost ohrožených obojživelníků, plazů, rostlin a hmyzu, jejich biotopy a ekosystémy.
 Aplikovaný výzkum – spolupracujeme s veřejnými institucemi, neziskovými organizacemi a správci chráněných území na efektivní ochraně přírody.
 Projekty LIFE – jsme aktivní součástí evropského programu LIFE, který podporuje udržitelnost a ochranu přírodních lokalit.

Proč to děláme:
Naším cílem je porozumět přírodním procesům, přispět k udržitelné ochraně přírody a aktivně obnovovat cenná stanoviště. Kombinujeme moderní vědecké poznatky s praktickými opatřeními, která přinášejí výsledky v terénu i ve strategickém plánování ochrany přírody.

Spojte se s námi:
Máte zájem o spolupráci, odborné konzultace nebo více informací o našich aktivitách? Navštivte naše webové stránky www.envirop.cz nebo nás kontaktujte přímo zde na LinkedIn.

Name: Women in Nature Conservation (Long Island)
Description:
Our mission is to foster a community where women can connect, collaborate, and drive positive change to address pressing environmental challenges using their unique perspectives, skills & passion. 

Our approach is two-fold.  

Community Building: Cultivating a supportive and inclusive network where women from diverse backgrounds can come together to share knowledge, experiences, and resources. By fostering strong connections, we aim to create a sense of belonging that empowers each member to contribute effectively to environmental initiatives.

Education and Advocacy: Empowering women with the knowledge and skills needed to become influential advocates for environmental sustainability. Through workshops, seminars, and collaborative projects, we strive to enhance our members' understanding of key environmental issues and equip them with the tools to drive positive change in their communities.

Together, we aim to create a powerful force for environmental change, demonstrating that women play a vital role in shaping a sustainable and resilient future for all. Through collaboration, education, and action, our network strives to be a catalyst for positive environmental transformation led by women.

Name: Old Woman Creek National Estuarine Research Reserve
Description:
Old Woman Creek National Estuarine Research Reserve (NERR) is managed as a cooperative partnership between the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) and ODNR. Old Woman Creek NERR is managed by the ODNR Office of Coastal Management; it is also a dedicated State Nature Preserve, managed by the ODNR Division of Natural Areas and Preserves.

Located in Huron on the south-central shore of Lake Erie, Old Woman Creek is one of Ohio's few remaining examples of a natural estuary. As a transition zone between land and water, the site contains a variety of habitats including marshes and swamps, upland forest, open water, tributary streams, barrier beach and near-shore Lake Erie. The Reserve supports a diverse assemblage of native plants and animals representative of freshwater estuaries.

The Reserve’s administrative offices and Visitor Center are located in the Mike DeWine Center for Coastal Wetland Studies, which serves as a focal point for public visitation and education programs. The Center provides laboratories for ecological research, a classroom for education, and a variety of interactive displays.

Designated in 1980, Old Woman Creek NERR is part of a national network of 30 State-Federal partnership coastal reserves that address state and regional coastal management needs through research, education, and stewardship. The NERR System is administered by NOAA in collaboration with State partners and uses its network of living laboratories to help understand and find solutions to crucial issues facing America’s coastal communities. Each of the 30 reserves, including Old Woman Creek, is comprised of four common sectors: Education, Research, Stewardship, and Training.

Name: Gulf Coast Environmental Engagement Center (GCEEC)
Description: Welcome to the Gulf Coast Environmental Equity Center (GCEEC), an initiative proudly established at the University of South Alabama (USA) in collaboration with Dauphin Island Sea Lab (DISL). Our center is dedicated to fostering environmental justice, supporting impactful research, and promoting sustainable stewardship within Alabama's coastal communities. As a pass-through entity for a $1.75 million competitive subaward program, we focus on empowering local projects that address water quality, habitat restoration, and community resilience.

Name: EcoAddendum
Description: EcoAddendum, known as Eco-A,  provides environmental education and reconnects diverse individuals, communities, and municipal leaders to the natural environment.  Our specialty is guided Naturalist Walks and outings to little known natural places around the southeast.  We also specialize in environmental stewardship training, volunteer activities focusing on removing invasive plants from parks and other public places, greenspace plant inventories and mapping, and forest restoration plans.

Name: Réseau des Initiatives Agroécologiques au Maroc
Description: Pour faire face aux défis de la dégradation des patrimoines nourriciers et de l’insécurité alimentaire, le RIAM a été créé officiellement en 2015. Le Réseau des Initiatives Agroécologiques au Maroc cherche à établir les liens entre les acteurs à travers le partage d’information et la mutualisation des ressources. A cet effet, le RIAM soutiens les initiatives agroécologiques en leur offrant visibilité, formation et accompagnement, tout en sensibilisant la société civile en matière d’alimentation saine et durable. Pour promouvoir ces objectifs, nous sollicitons les valeurs d’humanisme, du respect du vivant et la solidarité. Pour cela, le réseau organise des forums, développe un Système Participatif de Garantie (SPG) et incite à la coopération entre ses membres autour d’initiatives concrètes telles que la permaculture et l’agriculture urbaine.

Name: Planning Ahead
Description:
Planning Ahead is a community-centered organization advancing climate resilience, environmental education, and systems change. We design and deliver programs that help communities move from being impacted by climate risks to being prepared, informed, and in influence.

Our work centers lived experience as expertise. We partner with residents, nonprofits, municipalities, and institutions to co-create climate adaptation strategies that are practical, equitable, and built to last.

Name: WILDLIFE CLUBS OF UGANDA Official
Description:
Wildlife Clubs of Uganda (WCU) is a national, non-religious, and non-political association dedicated to promoting environmental and wildlife conservation among young people.

History and Mission
Founded in 1975, WCU works with schools across Uganda to educate and inspire the next generation of conservationists. The organisation's core mission is to "educate the population to ensure the sustainable use of Uganda's natural resources and to conserve the country's wildlife heritage for the present and future generations."

WCU's key objectives include:

Cultivating a desire for environmental conservation among Ugandans and East Africans.

Promoting the appreciation and scientific study of Uganda's wildlife and environment.

Highlighting the economic, recreational, and aesthetic value of the country's natural resources.

Fostering cooperation with other organisations that share similar conservation goals.

Activities
WCU implements its mission through a variety of programs and activities, many of which are school-based. These include:

Conservation Education: Leading conservation education to help young people understand nature and use resources wisely.

Tree Planting: Implementing tree planting and ecosystem restoration projects, with a focus on indigenous and agroforest trees.

School Clubs: Forming and supporting wildlife clubs in schools to raise awareness and promote active participation in conservation.

Events and Programs: Organising annual competitions, wildlife club conventions, mobile education initiatives, wildlife camps, and conservation seminars and workshops.

Publications: Producing and distributing educational materials such as magazines and newsletters.

Citizen Science: Engaging members in citizen science programs to promote scientific study of the environment.

Membership
WCU's membership has expanded beyond school groups to include honorary members, associate members, adult groups, and corporate members.

Name: EUTOPIA - Interreg
Description:
De projectenportefeuille EUTOPIA, onder leiding van de Provincie West-Vlaanderen en het Département du Nord, brengt 35 projectpartners en betrokken stakeholders samen rond één gezamenlijke ambitie: de Frans-Vlaamse regio voorbereiden op een klimaatbestendige toekomst.
Binnen het Interreg VI Frankrijk–Wallonië–Vlaanderen programma bundelt de projectenportefeuille de krachten van vijf grensoverschrijdende projecten — Clim@Dunes, Clim@YserAa, Clim@Monts, Clim@TouVert en Clim@Villes — die samenwerken aan waterbeheer, biodiversiteit, groene infrastructuur en veerkrachtige steden en landschappen.

La portefeuille de projets EUTOPIA, coordonnée par la Province de Flandre occidentale et le Département du Nord, réunit 35 partenaires et acteurs associés autour d’une ambition commune : préparer la région franco-flamande à un avenir résilient face au climat.
Dans le cadre du programme Interreg VI France–Wallonie–Flandre, la portefeuille de projets rassemble cinq projets transfrontaliers — Clim@Dunes, Clim@YserAa, Clim@Monts, Clim@TouVert et Clim@Villes — qui coopèrent autour de la gestion de l’eau, de la biodiversité, des infrastructures vertes et de la résilience des villes et des paysages.

Name: People and the city
Description:
People and the City | About Us
We believe water is life — and lakes are a chance to reconnect with nature, ourselves, and our cities.
People and the City is a non-profit initiative working to transform Bulgaria’s neglected urban and peri-urban lakes into safe, accessible, and vibrant public bathing spaces.

Through research, fieldwork, workshops, festivals, and public competitions, we promote sustainable use of natural water bodies and advocate for long-term landscape transformations — inspired by successful practices in Germany and the UK.

 Our goals:

Replace the sign “Swimming Prohibited” with “Welcome to Your Bathing Lake”

Promote safety, lifeguard infrastructure, and accessibility

Activate communities through temporary events and long-term redesign

Build strong partnerships with local governments, NGOs, schools, and artists

From site analysis and public advocacy to student involvement and prototype design, we create spaces for recreation, play, and belonging — by the water, for the people.

 Join us in rethinking the lakes of Bulgaria. Let’s make them alive again — together.

Градове за хората | За нас
Вярваме, че водата е живот, а езерата – шанс.
Шанс за връзка с природата, за отдих, за грижа и общност.

„Градове за хората“ е сдружение с нестопанска цел, чиято цел е да трансформира изоставените градски и крайградски езера в България в безопасни, достъпни и оживени къпални зони.

Чрез теренни проучвания, работилници, конкурси, фестивали и междуинституционални партньорства, насърчаваме устойчивото използване на водоемите и работим за дългосрочни ландшафтни трансформации – вдъхновени от добрите практики в Германия и Великобритания.

 Нашата мисия:

Да заменим табелата „Къпането забранено“ с табела „Добре дошли при езерото на хората“

Да създадем безопасна инфраструктура със спасителни постове и зони за отдих

Да ангажираме гражданите чрез временни намеси и летни събития

Да изградим силни партньорства с общини, институции, училища и артисти

Name: The New Forest | Network per la valorizzazione dell'Appennino Parmense-Piacentino
Description:
Nell'ambito del progetto PNRR Ecosister, la Sfida “The New Forest” intende creare un Network di proprietari inclusivo (es. tramite Accordi di Foresta) e di altri Stakeholder, che valorizzi maggiormente il patrimonio boschivo dell’Appennino Parmense-Piacentino. Per raggiungere una massa critica minima di proprietari, è fondamentale il supporto di Enti Pubblici e Chiesa (anche in quanto proprietari), Scuole, Imprese, Associazioni e Comunità tutta. Valorizzare la Foresta e il Legname, permetterà di aumentare la sostenibilità delle Foreste; tutelare la biodiversità, limitando il dissesto idrogeologico; limitare lo spopolamento creando nuovi posti di lavoro.

L’idea potrebbe essere quella del cambio di destinazione d’uso della legna da ardere (es. legno da opera), prevedendo poi la valorizzazione degli Scarti di Produzione e/o quelli derivanti dalla normale “manutenzione” boschiva, in nuove tipologie di combustibile (es. Idrogeno Verde) e/o Biochar. A supporto dell’idea principale, si valuterà anche la vendita di crediti di carbonio e/o di sostenibilità, al fine di avere ponte di unione tra i proprietari boschivi, in aggiunta ad altri strumenti già previsti dalla SNAI quali gli “Accordi di Foresta”. Valorizzando la Foresta, si potrà al contempo sostenere altri settori, come l’Agricoltura, l’Allevamento, lo Sport, il Turismo Lento ed Esperienziale, l’Ospitalità Diffusa, la Bio-Architettura, il Trasporto, ecc.

Gli obiettivi prefissati sono:
- Creazione di network: Privati, Enti Pubblici, Imprese e Associazioni No Profit (APS) per la valorizzazione del patrimonio boschivo dell’Appennino Parmense-Piacentino.

- Sviluppo della Metro-Montagna: Relazioni tra i territori montani e metropolitani limitrofi basati sulla cooperazione reciproca per lo sviluppo di servizi e benessere collettivo.

- Valorizzazione dei servizi ecosistemici: Benefici multipli forniti dagli ecosistemi al genere umano come approvvigionamento, valori culturali, supporto alla vita e regolazione.

Name: Texas Institute for Applied Environmental Research
Description:
The Texas Institute for Applied Environmental Research (TIAER) at Tarleton State University, part of the Texas A&M University System, was established in 1991 by the Texas Legislature. TIAER focuses on addressing nonpoint source pollution through initiatives like Industry-Led Solutions, funded by the U.S. EPA.

TIAER excels in mobilizing stakeholders to develop and implement watershed protection plans, Total Maximum Daily Loads (TMDLs), and use attainability analyses (UAAs). Their expertise has driven significant water quality improvements, using the North Bosque River as a key research site and extending efforts across Texas, the U.S., and globally.

With a diverse team of environmental scientists, agricultural economists, and public relations experts, TIAER effectively engages stakeholders and tackles critical environmental challenges.

Name: Aluminium Loop
Description:
Aluminium Loop represents a mission to create the world’s most environmentally sustainable recycling loop.

We know that aluminium cans and bottles are already a really environmentally friendly form of packaging, and we believe the sky is the limit for this product.
Aluminium Loop was founded to push the boundaries of this strong  foundation and create a more impactful positive change.

We have worked tirelessly to create the unrivalled infrastructure in Thailand,  representing one of only six  countries in the world with the full recycling capabilities for aluminium cans.  Furthermore, the infrastructure of Aluminium Loop is geographically concentrated allowing for minimal freight costs, shorter recycling times and the ability to provide just-in-time supply across all partners.  Finally, Thailand has a high collection rate for used beverage cans due to the economic available for waste sorters. 

We continue to push this loop to next level by driving our partners toward net zero. 

We are committed, ready, and excited for what is to come-and the most important thing is, we are only just getting started.

Description:
There are over 200 manufacturers of Electric Scooters, 120 manufacturers of Electric Bicycles, Ecargo Bikes suppliers are growing rapidly too. In addition there are myriad Light Electric Vehicle manufacturers and dozens of Electric Van types. Electric England too have colleagues looking after Electric Helicopters, Electric Speedboats, Electric Trash Collection Lorries and almost every production car manufacturer has at least one Electric version on the road or on the horizon.

So when looking for a way to improve a company's carbon footprint, or reduce the level of carbon emissions caused by its internal combustion engine vehicles, every company has thousands of potential models to choose from. 

But who is the right person, at the right company to talk to for such a fundamentally important change?

The manufacturer? We don't think so.

Companies want to sell their brand of product, and their brand only, regardless of its degree of suitability to a specific and unique set of needs.

Who should be the first contact with then?

We should, Electric England Ltd, we fit perfectly in to this space. With free and expert fact finding visits from our specialist consultants, your pain points and priorities, impacts and aspirations can all be identified and addressed and a bespoke problem solving proposition created. 

Each and every week our team of unbiased and objective experts meet with new innovators and inventors, have discussions with global and local manufacturers. We invite to our service centre experts from around the World, to review, to understand, and to become expert in the latest technologies. 

Let Electric England  guide you through the maze of clean transportation and also the rapidly growing number of supporting products and services for them, from the UK and all over the World.

The Electric England network of local experts are with you from the very beginning and throughout. 

With expert Planning, Installation, Service, Maintenance and Support since 2020.

Name: Het Groene Zuiden
Description: Het Groene Zuiden verbindt, informeert, betrekt en stimuleert wijkbewoners om samen te werken aan een groener en energiezuiniger Zwolle-Zuid.

Name: In Horto Cerasorum
Description: Met het project In Horto Cerasorum wil onze nieuwe vzw kinderen niet alleen bewonderend leren kijken naar de pracht van de natuur in al zijn seizoenen, maar ze ook laten participeren: kersen plukken, dieren voederen, eieren rapen… Zo leren ze genietend dat we ons gedrag moeten bijsturen als we de plek waar wij wonen leefbaar willen houden. En we laten hen ook een boeiend facet van onze stad ontdekken: de fruitpluk en -handel.

Name: TRANS-LIPO Interreg (Biocontrol 4.0)
Description: TransLipo aims to develop sustainable biocontrol solutions using biosourced lipopeptides (Iturin, Fengycin, Surfactin) and glycolipids (e.g. rhamnolipids) to combat major fungal diseases by enhancing plant defense mechanisms and antifungal activity. The project focuses on key crop-pathogen systems—wheat–Septoria, potato–downy mildew, leek/onion–downy mildew, and apple–scab—to assess efficacy, formulation, environmental impact, and mode of action under both controlled and field conditions.

Name: Western Canada SFI Implementation Committee (WCSIC)
Description:
The Western Canada Sustainable Forestry Initiative Implementation Committee (WCSIC) is made up of Sustainable Forestry Initiative (SFI) Program Participants in British Columbia, Alberta and Saskatchewan. SFI Program Participants are SFI certified forest companies or organizations that believe forest landowners have an important stewardship responsibility and a commitment to society, and they recognize the importance of maintaining viable public and private forest forestlands.

SFI Program Participants support sustainable forestry practices on forestland they manage and promote it on other lands. They support efforts to protect private property rights, and to help all landowners manage their forestland sustainably.

The WCSIC performs local outreach and education activities that promote sustainable forestry, the SFI program, and SFI Standard conformance on public and private forestlands.

Name: Greenpact Planet
Description:
Greenpact Planet is a subscription platform that aims to connect you with the best climate solutions. We’ll lead you toward achieving your net zero goals

On greenpactplanet.com you can simultaneously support our partners and then track and visualize your Green Impact on our Planet through exclusive tools

Name: Collective for Climate, Environment, and Sustainability (CEST)
Description: A foundation established to facilitate research and become a strategic think tank for a sustainable equitable future. We focus on finding solutions and innovative initiatives for policy-making and practices in water, waste, sanitation and the environment, which impact the society.

Name: City Problem Solvers
Description:
Together we are... City Problem Solvers from all over working together to understand problems and how to solve them. 

If you have a problem, or know how to solve some problems, or want to know how to solve some problems, and you are interested in helping to be part of an organization that cares about the city, the environment, and the people that live in it, then you too can be a City Problem Solver!

City Problem Solvers creates an opportunity for community leaders and individuals to be involved in a collective, and meaningful effort to help out in the much needed work that needs to be done in the city to discover and problem solve important and overlooked city problems to improve quality of life for present and future generations of everyone living in the city.

Name: ONG Educ'Eco
AIHABs
Description: AIHABs is a multidisciplinary innovative initiative aimed at developing an integrated evaluation system to forecast the risk derived from the presence of emerging cyanotoxins in inland and coastal ecosystems. The innovation of this proposal resides at merging tools as last generation Artificial Intelligence (AI), remote sensing, nanosensors, hydrodynamic modelling and massive genetic sequencing with the joint purpose of providing an early warning system to decision making authorities in terms of risk to the population. The predicting modelling effort will allow a timely action to minimize the risks of consuming surface waters or using them as recreational resources when the waterbodies are prone to produce toxic cyanobacterial blooms.

Name: Clean Tunnel Air International AS
Description:
Introduction
CTA is a subsidiary company of Anderl Group, based in TRONDHEIM city of Norway. For over 20 years CTA has been a world leader in the development and provision of air purification for road tunnel s. Since 1989, CTA has done more than 20 air purification stations in more than 10 tunnels.

Company Mission:
To develop, manufacture and market total systems for control of air pollution in road tunnels.

Products & service:
CTA international specializes in designing, developing, manufacturing and installing turnkey electrostatic dust precipitation and gas removing systems. The company offers unique technology for minimizing space requirements in tunnel areas versus high efficiency.

Technology & Expertise
CTA areas of technology and expertise include:

In-depth knowledge of electrostatic filtration technology and applications
Unique technology for minimizing bypass tunnel areas through high-efficiency filtration and high speed air flow
Sophisticated waste water recycling system with integrated PLC to control the process automatically
Research & Development
CTA is working on the development of electrostatic filters for new areas of application in cooperation with other system suppliers. The company has projects in these areas in regards to gas cleaning in Spain and for filters for allergic or asthmatic people in Austria. In addition, CTA also cooperates with universities to evaluate improvements, and develop part of our R&D.
Milestone Cases
Festning (Oslo, Norway),1989,world's earlist air purification tunnel;
Chinbu Tunnel (South Korea),2001,the world's first air purification tunnel equipped with Waste Water Recycling System;
M30 Madrid Calle 30 Project, Madrid, Spain,2007,the world's largest air purification station in tunnel,with a air volume of 694 m3/s;
Central Kowloon Route,HK,2022,the world's largest tunnel air purification project, 9 air purification stations, with a total air volume of 2250 m3/s

Name: The Carbon Co
Description:
The Carbon Foundation is a compliance-led carbon credit development and certification business headquartered in Cyprus, with international operations and offices across the United Kingdom, United States, Albania, Canada and New Zealand. 

We help project owners convert real-world decarbonisation and climate-positive projects into high-integrity, audit-ready carbon credits that stand up to scrutiny and unlock real commercial value.

Our work spans the full lifecycle: 

/ Project origination and feasibility, 
/ Methodology alignment, 
/ MRV design and implementation, 
/ Verification readiness, 
/ Credit issuance, 
/ Registry infrastructure, 
/ Monetisation via structured offtake and market access. 

We are building a globally recognised, compliance-grade approach (not voluntary-led), designed to meet or exceed emerging requirements across regulated and Article 6-aligned markets. We are also progressing our ESYD accreditation pathway as part of our broader assurance and governance strategy.

What sets us apart is our proprietary technology stack, the Carbon Operating Platform (cOS) that supports rigorous project assessment, controls, reporting and transparency alongside optional tokenisation and digital traceability (including retirement controls) for stakeholders who require strong audit trails and data integrity.

We operate through structured country programmes, government and enterprise partnerships, and a specialist delivery network across policy, MRV, legal, and digital infrastructure, bringing credibility, speed, and accountability to every project we support.

If you’re seeking a trusted pathway to compliance-grade credits, we’d welcome a conversation.

Name: IPWE_Bhopal
Description: Welcome to Bhopal, the City of Lakes in Central India. Join us to explore a broad spectrum of water infrastructure themes pertinent to nations at various stages of development. The Environmental & Water Resources Institute (EWRI) of the ASCE with its 26,000 members and a tradition of successful International Perspectives on Water and Environment (IPWE) Conferences invites global engineers and scientists to Bhopal for its 12th session focusing on sustainable and resilient water infrastructure.

Name: Standards Assure FZE
Description: Unlocking Potential, Empowering Growth: We are your trusted partner in achieving ISO certification excellence. Seamlessly navigating the journey towards industry standards, we ensure sustainability and success for your business. Let's elevate your standards together!

Name: Projet TOUS'ECO
Description:
Prix du Concours Déclics Jeunes 2023 de la Fondation de France, le Projet TOUS'ECO est une initiative visant à promouvoir la transition écologique en République du Bénin, à travers l'éducation environnementale des enfants et des jeunes. 

D'une durée de 26 mois, ce projet a essentiellement pour cible,  les apprenants des Communes de Cotonou et Abomey-Calavi au Sud du Bénin. 

Le Projet est entièrement financé par la Fondation de France et reçoit l'appui technique de l'Institut de l'Engagement, de l'ONG Afrique Eco 2100 Bénin, ainsi que de l'Institut Africain pour le Développement de la Famille (IADF-ONG).

Name: Pacific Environment Vietnam (PE-VN)
Description:
Pacific Environment empowers communities to protect their well-being and the natural world around them. We get money and expertise to the ground in challenging venues on the Pacific Rim, including China, Russia, and the Alaskan Arctic. By strengthening communities and protecting the environment in such places, we also promote transparency, public participation, and legal enforcement, which form the basis on which people can achieve lasting protection of their health and the environment.

Pacific Environment offers one of the very few granting and training programs serving grassroots leaders around the Pacific Rim. At the heart of our long-term commitment to community-led environmental activism is a comprehensive support program. The community leaders and groups we partner with consistently tell us that the value we provide stems from three things: 1) giving them financial and mentoring support; 2) bringing them together in coordinated networks; and 3) supplying much needed expertise for their campaigns (e.g., technical, scientific, legal).   

Over three decades we have nurtured more than 150 grassroots organizations around the Pacific Rim and provided millions of dollars. In turn, the networks we foster create concentric ripples outward to many more people and organizations that ally together to undertake public education, litigation, media outreach, large-scale advocacy campaigns, and sophisticated policy work. 

To complement these community efforts, Pacific Environment has developed substantial expertise in global policy venues typically beyond the reach of local leaders. For example, we have obtained one of the few seats available to NGOs at the International Maritime Organization (IMO), a U.N. agency that writes the rules for the high seas. We use this seat to advocate for rules that protect marine mammals and indigenous food security, curb greenhouse gas emissions, and protect Arctic waters from the dangers of catastrophic oil spills.

Name: DSO2002
Description:
DSO2002 est la réponse aux nuisances olfactives provoquées par la production d'effluents gazeux en restauration professionnelle.
DSO2002 n'est ni un neutralisant, ni un masquant,  mais un véritable destructeur d'odeurs.

DSO2002 constitué notamment de groupements organiques détruit totalement les molécules malodorantes en créant des sels inertes non toxiques solubles dans l'eau.

Name: ADCPro
Description:
Spécialistes des mesures de niveaux des liquides ou solides, vitesses et débits des eaux par technologie Doppler, ADCPro distribue les courantomètres SonTek depuis 2008, et commercialise d'autres outils professionnels de marques internationales réputées telles que Pulsar, YSI et HYPACK.

En particulier, perches de jaugeages, débitmètres, échosondeurs et bathymètres, logiciels hydrographiques, bouées et sondes multiparamètres. 

En complément, notre gamme de drones de surface comprend :
	•	drones pour bathymétrie
	•	drones de prélèvements et d'analyse des eaux
	•	drones marins pour études hydrographiques
	•	drones sauveteurs

Dans le domaine industriel nos capteurs de niveau ultrasons et radar répondent, là aussi, aux demandes les plus exigeantes.

ADCPro (Acoustique Doppler Courantomètres Profileurs) est une entreprise 100 % Française, avec un personnel ayant en moyenne plus de 20 années d’expérience dans le domaine. Nos interlocuteurs nous comptent comme référents en matière d’instruments de mesures Doppler sur les cours d’eau, les eaux lacustres et maritimes.

Nous assurons la formation et le suivi de nos utilisateurs (support technique, maintenance, révision, SAV, évolution des solutions).

Notre politique d’entreprise se caractérise par une écoute attentive, une culture assidue de la qualité et une recherche de la performance.

Besoin d'une solution adaptée à vos mesures ? Contactez-nous !

Name: Lemonabroad
Description:
We make IT greener, wherever you are.

In today's interconnected world, information technology is the backbone of success. But traditional IT can come at a cost to the environment. That's where we come in. We are a leading provider of sustainable IT services, delivering eco-friendly solutions that help businesses thrive without sacrificing the planet.

Our global network of experts implements cutting-edge technologies designed to minimize your environmental footprint. We focus on:

Energy Efficiency: Optimizing hardware and software to reduce energy consumption and reliance on fossil fuels.
Cloud Optimization: Leveraging the power of the cloud to consolidate resources and reduce your physical footprint.
E-Waste Reduction: Implementing responsible disposal and recycling practices for retired IT equipment.
Sustainable Procurement: Helping you source energy-efficient and ethically manufactured technology.

Name: Avatar Lingkungan
Description: Avatar Lingkungan merupakan sebuah wadah anak muda untuk menjadi pionir perubahan khususnya dalam bidang lingkungan. Melalui berbagai project yang akan dilaksanakan kedepannya, diharapkan Avatar Lingkungan mampu membawa perbaikan serta berbagai solusi tentang persoalan lingkungan kepada masyarakat luas.

Name: Quality Parks
Description: Quality Parks extensive experience in conservation directs you to vetted experts and places.  We are a professional organization sponsoring projects that improve both the conservation business and existing park conditions (public access, stewardship, and more). We also grow, sell, and consult for native plants and plantings.

Description: Pour la protection de l'environnement...

Name: Spighe Verdi
Description: Spighe Verdi è il programma per lo sviluppo rurale sostenibile. Si rivolge ai Comuni che intendono valorizzare e investire sul proprio patrimonio rurale, migliorando le buone pratiche ambientali. Nel rapporto che si crea tra Comune, agricoltori e comunità locale sta il successo di Spighe Verdi, un programma flessibile e adattabile per promuovere buone pratiche ambientali in diversi contesti territoriali. Nel programma Spighe Verdi è centrale il ruolo che ha l’agricoltura nella difesa del paesaggio, nella tutela della biodiversità e nella produzione di alimenti di qualità. Il Comune, per ottenere la certificazione e il marchio Spiga Verde, deve autocandidarsi seguendo un protocollo in cui è prevista la rispondenza a criteri contenuti in diverse aree tematiche (acqua, energia, agricoltura, rifiuti, assetto urbanistico, tutela del paesaggio, ecc.). La candidatura è volontaria e gratuita e viene valutata da una Commissione di Valutazione nazionale che riunisce esperti provenienti da diversi enti pubblici e privati.

Name: Eko Liga
Description: Eko Liga to grywalizacja o tematyce ekologicznej, skierowana do pracowników firm. Gra jest połączeniem świetnej zabawy z wymiernymi korzyściami dla Twojej firmy i środowiska naturalnego.

Name: DIAGSOL Expertise Pollution des sols
Description:
Créé en 2003, DIAGSOL vous propose des diagnostics de pollution des sols qualitatifs et adaptés à vos besoins et problématiques.
Entreprise à taille humaine, jeune et dynamique, nous sommes en capacités d’intervenir sur la France avec réactivité et efficacité.

Nos missions :
Phase 1 & 2 - Informations / Diagnostics / Vérifications
Plan de Gestion
Evaluation Quantitative des Risques Sanitaires - EQRS
Sondages
Prélèvement d’échantillons
Parc matériel adapté: Sondeuse, PID, carottier battu, etc…

Depuis près de 20 ans Nous vous accompagnons sur tous vos projets dans le respect de la norme NF31-620

Diagsol est depuis sa création certifié OCEP (Organisation des consultants en environnement et pollution).

Name: Nu voor morgen / Présents pour le futur
Description:
Dialoog over de toekomst van radioactief afval.
Dialogue sur l'avenir des déchets radioactifs.

Name: PGS-Proficiency Global Services
Description: PGS Is a Quality And Proficiency Testing with environmental ,occupational health and safety services ,improvement methodologies.

Name: ReiCat Solutions S.r.l.
Description: Consulenza nell’ambito della realizzazione, dell’allestimento e della gestione di impianti chimico-tecnici industriali e distribuzione, anche sotto forma di procacciatore d’affari, di macchinari ed impianti tecnici industriali.

Name: UNICOT Malawi
Description:
UNICOT is a non-profit organization that empowers rural and urban communities to adopt sustainable practices that reduce environmental degradation. 

We envision a Malawi where people are thriving through sustainable environmental care and community development.

Name: The Plant Exchange
Description:
Started in a front yard in 2007 and with 75 attendees, 10 years later we draw 2000 participants, 250 volunteers where 3500 plants, tools and supplies change hands. The Plant Exchange encourages reuse, recycling and organic living. The flagship program is the free semi-annual event where gardeners, landscapers, urban farmers, and beginners come to exchange plants, equipment and information about ways to make our urban environment more sustainable, aesthetic, and healthy. 

The Plant Exchange creates strong neighborhood connections, encouraging green practices and support Oakland’s designation as one of the greenest cities in the country.

The Plant Exchange also hosts a Monthly Urban Gardening Speaker Series educating the community on gardening, tree pruning, soil, etc.

Name: ITI Consulting company
Description:
ITI is a forward-thinking company based in Bosnia and Herzegovina, committed to addressing environmental challenges through innovative, nature-based solutions. As a new player in the field, we bring a fresh perspective to solving issues that threaten natural ecosystems and endanger the environment.
Our team is composed of highly experienced international and national experts in biodiversity conservation, as well as nature and environment protection, who are dedicated to making a tangible impact on the ground.
We actively collaborate with both international and national civil society organizations (CSOs) and non-governmental organizations (NGOs) to strengthen local capacities and introduce advanced approaches that have been successfully implemented across Europe. Our partnerships are designed to not only enhance the effectiveness of our initiatives but also to provide local communities with the tools and knowledge they need to continue these efforts independently. By combining global expertise with local knowledge, ITI is at the forefront of driving sustainable environmental solutions in Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Vision
Our vision at ITI d.o.o. is to leave the environment in a better state than we found it by restoring and protecting the natural world and fostering growth and economic prosperity in harmony with nature.
Location: Novo Sarajevo, Federation of Bosnia and Herzegovina

Name: Evaluation Terrestre de l'Environnement
Description: Etudes d'impact environnemental, de dangers, risques, géotechnique, assainissement, management de déchets et audits environnementaux.

Name: iiCARE
Description: The Group has Programs Focusing on Renewable Energy and Reducing Greenhouse gas Emission.

Name: ATASANPE
Les Pics Verts de la Loire
Description:
Les Pics Verts de la Loire sont une association qui veut agir pour la protection de l’environnement à l’échelle des habitants des communes ligériennes.

Notre but et de sensibiliser et de réunir différents acteurs locaux pour agir concrètement.
Les adhérents jouent un rôle important de création et de participation aux projets de l’association. Ces projets et actions sont réalisés pour les adhérents mais aussi pour les non adhérents.
Nous proposons des actions autour du végétal, avec une action phare de plantation de micro forêt sur des terrains publiques et privés (mais ouverts). Mais aussi une randonnée/ découverte pour rencontrer et échanger avec d’autres associations sur la protection de notre environnement.

Nous réalisons des actions autour de l’alimentation et de l’énergie dans le but d’amener à une réflexion sur nos modes de consommation, en construisant, avec la low tech, des fours solaires et rocket stove par exemple.

Notre objectif à travers ces actions et d’inciter chacun à agir et se rendre compte que les changements sont à la portée de tous.

Name: Instituto de Formación Superior Ambiental y Forestal de Panamá
Fundacja Green Gaya
Description: Stwórz z nami lepszą przyszłość, dbając wspólnie o środowisko naturalne

Name: Fort Bend County Environmental Organization (FBCEO)
Description:
FBCEO's Mission is to advocate and promote 
clean air, water, land, health, fair education and 
economic strategies that influence policies to 
significantly reduce disparities in Fort Bend 
County.

Name: Lembaga Sertifikasi Profesi Kesehatan Lingkungan Indonesia
SEMA - Secretaria Municipal de Meio Ambiente de Belford Roxo
RIVO
Description:
RiVO (Residue In, Value Out) is een initiatief dat zich richt op het samenbrengen van vraag en aanbod op het gebied van reststromen.
De werkgroep focust zich op het in kaart brengen van reststoffen (residue) en waarde (value). Wat voor het ene bedrijf een reststroom is, kan voor het andere bedrijf een gewilde grondstof zijn.
RiVO heeft een duidelijke visie en opereert vanuit die hoedanigheid volledig op non-profit basis. RiVO helpt bedrijven om vraag en aanbod van afvalstromen inzichtelijk en toegankelijk te maken.
RiVO gaat Oosterhoutse bedrijven bij dit project betrekken én betrokken houden. Uiteindelijk streven gemeente Oosterhout en RiVO naar een aanpak om circulariteit voor bedrijven binnen de Oosterhoutse gemeentegrenzen mogelijk te maken met als resultaat:
GEEN AFVAL, MAAR GRONDSTOF

Name: Chemsbury Environment Solutions
Description:
Chemsbury, a renowned provider of water treatment solutions, delivers top-notch products crafted to the highest global standards by leading manufacturers. Beyond merely supplying equipment, Chemsbury offers comprehensive solutions for both industrial and residential water treatment needs.

Dedicated to advancing technology for resource optimization and efficiency, Chemsbury's commitment extends to improving the world we live in. Our sustainability efforts go beyond environmental conservation, encompassing social, cultural, and economic dimensions for the greater good. Upholding values of integrity and transparency, Chemsbury prioritizes long-term relationships with customers, providing quality solutions at fair prices. Our corporate ethos revolves around fostering trust, honesty, and integrity in every interaction.

Name: Manningtree Earth Festival
Description:
A FREE Earth festival for local people from the Manningtree district; to entertain, inspire, educate, and empower. Through celebrating and showcasing the positive action that difference makers in our little town are taking, we aim to form a community of like-minded people.
The festival will be fully inclusive with something for people of all ages, including live music, food and drink stalls, vendors of eco products, mindfulness and relaxation. There will be a full programme of activities and workshops to interest and engage people at all stages of their journey towards caring for the Earth. The festival is being organised by a group of volunteers from the Manningtree District Business Chamber, Plastic Free Manningtree Mistley and Lawford, PACE Manningtree, the Town and Parish Councils and The Manningtree Streetkeepers group. 
Through holding a Summer festival within walking and cycling distance from the town centre,  bus stop and train station, we hope to create a carbon neutral (or positive) experience for everyone which can be the role model for future events in our community.

Name: MONACO 3D
Description:
Tout a commencé à Monaco, avec une idée simple :
offrir un service anti-nuisibles fiable, humain et irréprochable.

Aujourd’hui, Monaco 3D accompagne de nombreux professionnels et particuliers dans la lutte contre les nuisibles – des cuisines de restaurants aux terrasses d’hôtels, en passant par les immeubles résidentiels.
Nous croyons qu’une bonne intervention, ce n’est pas seulement éliminer un problème.
C’est prévenir, protéger et rassurer.

Certifiés CEPA et fournisseurs brevetés, nous garantissons des interventions rapides, discrètes et conformes aux plus hauts standards d’hygiène et de sécurité.

Dératisation – Désinsectisation – Désinfection
24h/24 et 7j/7 à Monaco et ses alentours
Des solutions durables, pensées pour l’avenir

Name: Avantu Global
Description: Avantu is a Company that sources natural-based products that cater to the agricultural, mining, water management services, leisure, equine and manufacturing industries, to remediate wastewater and water issues, increase crop yields and repel irritating and harmful insects in a cost-effective, eco-friendly, sustainable way.

Name: I.S.T.
Description:
IST raccoglie l’insieme di esperienze e interazioni di differenti competenze che, con percorsi diversi,
provengono dalla progettualità della tecnologia ultrasuoni in collaborazione con US.I. Srl, Engineering
Innovativa impegnata nel settore della meccatronica industriale e del trattamento dei materiali.
Propone l’impiego del sistema brevettato DryFogS per l’utilizzo dei propri prodotti in modo
innovativo, unico e specificatamente studiato per ottenere il massimo risultato nei trattamenti
ambientali e delle superfici.
Propone inoltre, ad altri operatori e produttori, la tecnologia DryFogS per ottimizzare al meglio
l’efficacia dei propri prodotti e servizi nelle loro specifiche applicazioni.

Name: AntlerArmour
Description: AntlerArmour is a series of  eco-friendly ingredients combined to create a substance meant to repel deer from roads, while also not being identified by humans. AntlerArmour's long-lasting odor will prevent the need for constant replenishment and will continue to prevent the rapid increase in global warming.

Name: Carbonzero
Description: Our mission is to remove 20,000,000 tons of CO2 from the atmosphere every year. How? We’ve invented a patented operating system that uses Bitcoin to do just that. Join us this fall when we launch the world’s first carbonzero Bitcoin.

Name: Consultora Jenecheru
Description: Asesoramiento especializado en trámite de Licencias Ambientales de diferentes tipos de proyectos, #ConsultoraJenecheru cuenta con personal especializado de primer nivel en asesoramiento con resultados garantizados.
Location: Santa Cruz de la Sierra, Santa Cruz

Name: Ecosynergie Afrique
Description:
EcoSynergie est une entreprise engagée pour un développement durable en Afrique sub-saharienne
Nous avons pour ambition de trouver des solutions intégrées aux problèmes majeurs auxquels fait face l'Afrique sub-saharienne. Parmi ces problématiques, on retrouve la santé et le bien-être social et familial, la sous-alimentation et la sécurité alimentaire, la production durable, les changements climatiques et les énergies propres. À travers ses activités, EcoSynergie se donne pour mission d'impacter les Objectifs de Développement Durable (ODD) liés à ces problématiques.

Name: Sustainably Philly
Description:
Our research project aims to evaluate the extent to which corporations are contributing to pollution in Philadelphia, and how corporations can minimize this environmental impact. Based on our research, we have identified corporations' significant effect on pollution production to be our major problem. This 'organization' proposes solutions for corporations to take to resolve this issue, also bringing environmental awareness to the Philadelphia community. 
     Along with the analysis made regarding the influence of corporations on the local environment, this project aims to motivate individuals from all backgrounds to participate actively in civically responsible ways to address environmental remediation through an adoption of sustainable living methods. This page will include posts ranging from article studies on microplastic concentration in Philadelphia waterways to public opinions gathered through primary research on corporate environmental regulations to visual informational mediums (pamphlets, infographics, etc) which encourage citizens to actively help resolve the social issue of corporate-based environmental pollution.

Name: Primequalitysa

Description:
In recognition of the rich history of the land we inhabit, Azadi86 acknowledges our status as guests on the ancestral territories of the Amah Mutsun and Ohlone peoples, who have stewarded these lands for millennia with a profound connection to the Earth and an inherent harmony with nature.

Our mission at Azadi86 is to honor and perpetuate the legacy of indigenous land stewardship by promoting ecological harmony, social justice, and personal well-being. Guided by the principles of sustainability, regeneration, and the wisdom of the natural world, we aspire to co-create a world where all beings thrive in symbiosis with the Earth and each other.
