Name: BaseCamp Rugs
Description:
Basecamp Rugs is a brand built on a passion for sustainability, ethical craftsmanship, and mindful living. We design and produce premium GOTS-certified organic cotton yoga rugs, offering a natural, sustainable alternative to plastic yoga mats. Our rugs are inspired by nature, designed to deepen the connection between human beings and the earth, while ensuring the highest environmental and social standards throughout the production process.

Born from founder Mike Donaldson’s love for the original Prana yoga rug, Basecamp Rugs grew out of a desire to create a product that was not only good for yoga practice but also for the planet. During the pandemic, Mike started weaving his own rugs, which evolved into a commitment to creating a fair trade-certified production workshop in India, supporting local artisans with fair wages and ethical working conditions.

At Basecamp Rugs, we believe that the products we use in our wellness practice should reflect the values of wellness, sustainability, and fairness. Our rugs are designed to provide practitioners with an authentic, natural yoga experience that fosters connection—not insulation—from the earth beneath them.

We are driven by a vision to empower artisans, support sustainable materials, and offer eco-conscious alternatives to the yoga community. Our upcoming fair trade-certified workshop in Panipat, India, will not only produce beautiful, durable rugs but also uplift local communities through fair labor practices and environmentally responsible production processes.

Join us on this journey toward mindful living, where every yoga practice is a step toward a more sustainable and ethical world.

Name: Bhavana Network

We weave blockchain clarity with permaculture wisdom, funding agroforestry, renewable energy, circular-economy ventures, and eco-village infrastructure through the Bhavana Exchange and our soon-to-launch Bhavana.network…

Projects onboard with rigorous verification and on-chain monitoring, then issue credits and revenue-sharing tokens that let supporters track impact in real time while sharing in long-term value creation…

Alongside partners like Shiplo for sustainable logistics and ZenCredits for credit certification, we build open-source frameworks so thousands of mission-aligned initiatives can replicate success - turning planet-positive action into a compelling, scalable investment class…

Whether you are an investor seeking measurable ESG returns, a land steward ready to monetize regeneration, or a brand looking to offset supply-chain footprints while uplifting communities, Bhavana provides the platform, partnerships, and purpose-driven culture to bring your vision to life…

Name: Keep California Green and Golden, Inc
Description:
About Keep California Green and Golden, Inc.
Welcome to Keep California Green and Golden (KCGG), a passionate nonprofit founded by Paisley Robles, a senior at Palo Alto High School. Our mission is simple yet ambitious: to restore and preserve the breathtaking beauty of our home state, one piece of trash at a time.

Our Mission: Actionable Impact
At KCGG, we're dedicated to making a tangible impact through direct, measurable action. Our core goal is to remove 2 metric tons of trash each month, totaling an ambitious 24 metric tons every year, from California's most vulnerable and beloved natural areas. Based in the San Francisco Bay Area, we mobilize community members across the state to tackle this challenge head-on.

We achieve this through:
-- Regular Cleanup Days: We host organized cleanup events every other weekend, bringing together volunteers from all walks of life to make a real difference.
-- Targeted Efforts: We prioritize cleanups in ecologically sensitive areas like our baylands and coastlines, where pollution causes the most significant environmental damage.
-- Community Engagement: We believe collective action is powerful, empowering individuals, families, and groups to join us in protecting their local environments.

Our Vision: A Pristine Golden State
California is renowned for its diverse and stunning natural landscapes, from the majestic redwoods to the vibrant deserts, and crucially, our vital baylands and precious coastal regions. These areas are not just scenic backdrops; they are critical ecosystems, home to unique wildlife, and essential to the health and well-being of our communities. Unfortunately, pollution threatens these treasures daily. We envision a pristine Golden State, free from the blight of trash, where nature can flourish, and communities can thrive in clean, healthy surroundings.

Ready to help us keep California green and golden? Join us at our next cleanup!
501c3 and EIN# 39-2696889

Name: Association MHD
Description:
Savez vous que vous gaspillez 10% de carburant ? il est possible de conditionner vos carburants pour obtenir une combustion totale, réduire la pollution et diminuer vos consommation.

La technologie MHD est utilisée par les pétroliers dans les installations de raffinage et de transport de carburant. Elle consiste à déstabiliser le carburant pour les opérations de production et de transport.

Depuis plus de 10 ans notre association recherche et développe ce fuel tuning pour les consommateurs de la région PACA, dans le but précis de faciliter l'échange avec l'air et la combustion.

Cette technologie n'a rien à voir avec les machines (moteurs, fours, chaudières, groupes froids) ce qui explique qu'elle fonctionne sur TOUS vos consommateurs. Nous ne touchons pas aux machines, nous nous contentons de les alimenter avec un carburant préparé pour brûler plus complètement.
Cette technologie n'a rien à voir avec les carburants : vous gardez vos fournisseurs habituels, nous préparons n'importe quel carburant du commerce.

Si vous êtes professionnel et que vous suivez vos consommations, nous vous payons pour vous apporter la preuve de l'efficacité de notre travail. 

Consultez-nous !

Name: Treefy
Description:
Treefy is the mobile app that transforms everyday habits into real, measurable climate impact.
Our mission is simple: make environmental action accessible, rewarding and part of daily life.

With Treefy, users plant real trees around the world through small, engaging micro-actions—such as completing daily tasks, staying active, watching rewarded ads, and inviting friends. Every tree contributes to certified reforestation projects that restore ecosystems, support local communities and increase global biodiversity.

To maximize transparency and trust, Treefy integrates with verified planting partners and provides clear impact tracking: users can follow their planted trees, monitor monthly progress and compete in leaderboards designed to boost motivation and long-term engagement. A smart referral system and a balanced reward economy ensure organic growth without compromising sustainability or authenticity.

Treefy is more than an app—it’s a growing movement. Our gamified approach turns environmental responsibility into a positive, social and rewarding experience. Whether users unlock their first tree or contribute hundreds over time, every action brings tangible benefits to the planet.

As we expand, Treefy aims to become a leading platform combining behavioral science, mobile engagement and environmental action, building partnerships with NGO networks, ethical brands and global sustainability initiatives.

Together, we’re making reforestation scalable, transparent and accessible to everyone, one tree at a time.

GreenFish Youth Initiative (GYI) is a youth-led, non-profit organization established in 2023 to address critical environmental degradation, food insecurity, and youth unemployment in Northern Uganda. Our approach integrates climate-smart agriculture, aquaculture, ecosystem restoration, and youth entrepreneurship, enabling communities to improve their livelihoods while conserving natural resources.

The communities we serve face rapid deforestation, declining soil fertility, unreliable rainfall patterns, and limited income opportunities. 
As a result:
Food insecurity affects over 40% of households.
 Youth unemployment is widespread, leading to rural poverty and migration.
 Natural resources, including wetlands and forests, are severely degraded.

These challenges threaten long-term food production and community resilience against climate change.

Name: BSF Initiative LTD
Description:
Révolutionner la gestion des déchets organiques et l'indépendance alimentaire à Maurice avec BSF Initiative !

Chez BSF Initiative Mauritius, nous sommes fiers de présenter une solution naturelle et durable pour transformer les défis environnementaux et économiques de l'île Maurice en opportunités.

Le Défi :
   30-40% des déchets à Maurice sont organiques.
   Forte dépendance aux importations d'aliments pour animaux (70-80%).
   Pollution des côtes par les engrais chimiques et coûts élevés de gestion des déchets.

Notre Solution : Les Larves de Mouche Soldat Noire (Hermetia Illucens) !
Ces larves voraces transforment les biodéchets en biomasse riche en protéines et nutriments, à travers un processus naturel et écologique.

Les Bénéfices Clés de notre Économie Circulaire :
    Réduction des Déchets : Jusqu'à 80% du volume des déchets organiques valorisé sur place.
    Fertilisants Naturels : Production de compost de haute qualité (Frass) pour l'agriculture locale.
    Indépendance Alimentaire : Création d'une source alternative et locale de protéines pour l'alimentation animale, réduisant notre dépendance aux importations.
    Impact Économique : Réduction des coûts de traitement pour les hôtels et création de valeur localement.

Nous sommes engagés à bâtir une île Maurice plus verte et plus résiliente. Nous travaillons en lien avec le FAREI (Ministry of Agriculture) et le secteur privé (hôtels, golfs, agriculture) pour un modèle "Gagnant-Gagnant-Gagnant".

Nous recherchons des partenaires et des opportunités de collaboration pour étendre notre impact. Rejoignez-nous dans cette mission durable !

Name: Airwise System
Description:
Airwise System, a Prime Superiority venture, is a next-generation urban platform designed to improve air quality and reshape the health and sustainability of modern cities. By integrating AI, advanced air monitoring sensors, IoT connectivity, and urban planning tools, Airwise delivers real-time, actionable insights for traffic optimization, pollution mitigation, and green space development.
We're not just measuring air quality — we're predicting it. Airwise System detects, classifies, and models air pollutants by source and nature, enabling municipalities to act before pollution becomes a crisis.
Industry
Smart Cities
Environmental Monitoring
AI & Data Analytics
Urban Planning & Infrastructure
GovTech
Our Core Innovations
Multi-Criteria Air Pollution Detection: Unique sensors identify pollutants based on physical, chemical, and biological characteristics — including noise, CO2, NOx, lead, hydrocarbons, and industrial soot.
Predictive Modeling: Our proprietary AI and machine learning algorithms can forecast air pollution concentrations up to 3 days in advance with over 83% accuracy.
Urban Integration: Seamless synchronization with municipal systems using IoT and Big Data enables real-time adjustments in traffic flow, land use, and green zone planning.
Privacy by Design: A fully closed-loop data system guarantees maximum privacy and security with no personal surveillance or sensitive data tracking.
We offer:
SaaS Solutions for city governments and smart city integrators.
Data-as-a-Service (DaaS) for environmental agencies and academic institutions.
Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) through long-term public-private partnerships.
Consulting & Analytics for urban development and green transformation.
Why Airwise System?
 Reduces public health risks
 Avoids environmental fines
 Enhances city attractiveness
 Boosts public transport usage

Name: Clairity
Description:
Clairity provides objective, sensor-driven insight into indoor air quality for schools, offices, and commercial buildings. Using portable, self-contained monitoring devices, Clairity captures continuous CO₂, VOC, temperature, and humidity data at the room level—even without building Wi-Fi or IT integration. This enables facility teams to identify ventilation issues, validate occupant concerns, and make data-based decisions that improve comfort, productivity, and operational efficiency.

The platform delivers high-resolution IAQ data, automated analytics, and clear visualizations that highlight trends, anomalies, and actionable findings. Clairity supports short-term audits, long-term monitoring, and district-wide or portfolio-wide environmental health assessments. By reducing guesswork and providing evidence-backed insight, Clairity helps organizations create healthier, more efficient indoor environments.

Name: BJE Environmental Optics
Description: BJE Environmental Optics (est. 2017) (BJE) is developing two scientific instruments that measure particles (aerosols) in the atmosphere, as well as consulting on atmospheric lidar (laser radar) technology.  Aerosols in the atmosphere are complicated varying widely in size, composition, shape, and distribution. The two instruments under development measure light scattered from aerosols and infer their properties.  Both instruments have been operated for research for several years, and one, the Imaging Polar Nephelometer (IPN), is patented.  The NOAA/SBIR effort will develop the instruments so that they can be easily commercialized by an established instrument company.  BJE expertise includes CCD cameras, wide-angle lenses, lasers, image processing, lidar electronics, optical detectors, and atmospheric analysis.

Name: Apache - Barrier for clean air
Description:
Traffic congestion isn't just a headache – it's a health hazard.

The APACHE project, (short fot: Aerodynamic, Pedestrian Level, Air quality Control using Urban Vegetation Elements ) will use computer modelling to spearhead research into new air pollution barriers.

Many of us already appreciate seeing greenery in our urban environments, but what if nature could also provide a solution for the growing issue of air pollution we’re facing in our cities?

The aim of APACHE is to create a barrier to pollutants that would be made partly of vegetation, and partly of solid material. With the right shape and characteristics, this barrier would direct air flow and pollutants from traffic away from pedestrians and residents without using any complex processes or extra energy. The porous, sponge-like nature of the vegetation and the combination of different materials would enable innovation in the development of such a barrier, described in engineering terms as a Nature Based Solution (NbS).

Focus will be placed on mitigating pollutant concentrations in the pedestrian zone at heights above ground relevant to children and adults.

The project will use a combination of computer modelling and wind tunnel experiments to develop and optimise the aerodynamic design. The team will also work with citizens and stakeholders in a co-creative process, taking into account the feedback of locals to ensure the product is as beneficial as possible for the community.

The project is funded by the Malta Council for Science and Technology under the Fusion Smart Cities programme. The principal investigator of the project is Dr Ing. Daniel Micallef while the project partner is Dr Edward Duca.

Name: ECO CLUB
Description:
The Eco Club at Pharo Secondary School in Sheikh has a broader vision that extends beyond the campus, aiming to foster a culture of environmental awareness and sustainability within the local community and the region at large. Established in 2016, the club actively engages in a variety of initiatives that not only educate students but also inspire them to take action in preserving the environment.

One of the club's key initiatives is organizing community-wide events, such as tree planting, environmental conservation, and climate change advocate in the Somaliland. These events highlight the importance of reforestation and the need to combat deforestation. By involving local residents, the Eco Club emphasizes the shared responsibility of environmental conservation, encouraging people of all ages to take part in sustainability efforts.

The Eco Club collaborates with various organizations and groups, including agricultural teams, government agencies, and local businesses. These partnerships help amplify their environmental campaigns and make a significant impact on the region’s sustainability. Through joint activities, such as workshops and outreach programs, the club ensures that its message reaches a wide audience, from students to community leaders.

Furthermore, the Eco Club’s involvement in larger environmental causes aligns with global efforts, such as the observance of World Environment Day. By participating in such global movements, the club not only educates its members but also contributes to the broader environmental conversation, linking local actions to global environmental goals.

In sum, the Eco Club at Pharo Secondary School serves as an influential force for environmental advocacy, not only within the school but also within the larger community of Sheikh and beyond, promoting sustainable living practices for a better future.

Through research, innovation, community engagement and system-level thinking, we aim to create sustainable pathways that restore ecological balance, human well-being and a healthy ecosystem.

We bring together diverse expertise from literature, social science, environmental science, psychology, sustainable economics and long-term thinking to catalyze eco-centred transformation. By bridging eco-conscious knowledge, practice and compassion, we empower youth to lessen the rift between communities and nature.

With the introduction of traditional designs of regenerative models, we strive to be a catalyst for lasting impact, where growth is redefined not as extraction, but mutual care; not as consumption, but regeneration.

If you believe in a future where humanity and nature evolve together, we invite you to connect with us. Let’s co-create the symbiotic existence on this living Gaia.

Name: Chattanooga-Hamilton County Air Pollution Control Bureau
Description:
Mission Statement
The Air Pollution Control Bureau, founded in 1969, is a quasi-governmental agency that strives to administer, and enforce, local air pollution control laws that are intended to achieve, maintain and exceed such levels of air quality as will protect human health and safety and to the greatest degree practicable, prevent injury to plant and animal life and property, and foster the comfort and convenience of the people.  

 

General Information
The Board of Directors guides the Bureau’s efforts. This board is made up of 10 members: three county-appointed members, three city-appointed members, three joint-agency members, and a representative from the Chattanooga-Hamilton County Health Department.

The Bureau’s day-to-day operations are carried out by a 13 member staff.  We have three departments: Air Monitoring, Engineering, and Administrative.

The Bureau has an exciting history, having played a major role in taking Chattanooga from the most polluted city in the nation, to one known for its revitalization and outdoor activities.  Chattanooga has been labeled as one of the most livable cities in the nation and we are proud of the Bureau’s and the citizens’ continuous efforts to keep our city that way.

Name: BioNota
Description:
BioNota | Investing in Nature, for Biodiversity, transparent Onchain 

BioNota is a climate-tech company transforming biodiversity into a transparent, investable asset class. We turn ecologically valuable land into Biodiversity Units (BDUs) digitally tokenized Real-World Assets (RWAs) that generate measurable impact, long-term financial value, for owners, investors and better futures for all.

Each BDU represents about 1 hectare of land legally secured and managed for ecological restoration over a minimum 10-year period. Through blockchain-based smart contracts, BDUs become transparent, traceable, and tradeable instruments, opening the door for individuals, companies, and institutional investors to directly co-finance the protection of our planet’s ecosystems.

From wetlands and forests to regenerative landscapes, BDUs unlock new funding streams from:
- Voluntary & compliance carbon markets
- Biodiversity credits and conservation incentives
- Government nature financing and ESG-aligned capital
- Onchain staking, trading, and yield opportunities via DeFi/ReFi platforms

We operate at the intersection of:
- Biodiversity finance
- Nature-based solutions (NbS)
- Blockchain infrastructure
- Impact investing & ESG

 Our model aligns with the Kunming-Montreal Global Biodiversity Framework, SDGs, TNFD, and emerging biodiversity net gain (BNG) policies.

 By tokenizing and securing real land-based impact, BioNota is building the infrastructure for the next evolution of conservation: programmable ecology.

Join us in making biodiversity a new asset class, accessible, accountable, and regenerative by design.

#InvestInNature #BioNota #BiodiversityFinance #ClimateTech #RWA #ReFi #ESG #NaturePositive #BlockChain #NFT

Name: The Choccolocco Creek Watershed
Description:
The Choccolocco Creek Watershed is located in East Alabama between Birmingham and Atlanta, and is one of the largest tributaries in the Coosa River drainage system.  Spanning four counties (Cleburne, Calhoun, Talladega, and Clay), the watershed covers more than 246,000 acres and drains approximately 376 square miles of land.  The headwaters originate in the Talladega National Forest at the foothills of the Appalachian Mountains and the creek flows southwesterly to its point of confluence with Lake Logan Martin.  

The general land use of the Choccolocco Watershed area consists of forest, row crop, pasture, and urban lands. Forestland and row crop are by far the most predominant lands that drain into Choccolocco Creek, however over one third of urban land drains directly into Choccolocco Creek.
The Choccolocco Creek Watershed hosts rain barrel & rain garden workshops, and has partnered with many area organizations to sponsor outdoor education activities in the Choccolocco Watershed. These area events include Get Outdoors Day, Classroom in the Forest, Worlds of Work, and Earth Day.

The Choccolocco Creek Watershed sponsors creek clean-up days each year at several locations in the watershed. Every year we remove thousands of glass bottles, plastic, styrofoam and other debris from the creek and surrounding riparian corridor.

Our office offers services and publications to assist with education and outreach efforts.  These services include field days and classroom presentations about the watersheds and water pollution using the Enviroscape model, Learning through Legacy materials, and Beetles.

Name: Texans For Environmental Awareness
Description:
At Texans For Environmental Awareness, we are dedicated to fostering environmental stewardship and proactive community engagement throughout the state of Texas. Our mission is to educate, empower, and mobilize Texans to advocate for sustainable practices and policies that protect our natural resources and enhance the quality of life for all residents.

Our Focus:

-Policy Advocacy: We work closely with policymakers to advocate for environmental legislation that ensures long-term ecological health and sustainability.

-Research and Education: By conducting and disseminating research on local environmental issues, we equip communities with the knowledge needed to make informed decisions and take effective action.

-Sustainable Practices: We champion sustainable living practices by showcasing innovative solutions and supporting businesses and initiatives that prioritize ecological balance.

Join Us:
Texans For Environmental Awareness invites all who care about Texas’s environmental future to join us. Together, we can make a significant impact and ensure a thriving, sustainable environment for generations to come.

Name: Carmel Caring Complex Ltd (3C)
Description:
Carmel Caring Complex Ltd. (3C) Overview

Carmel Caring Complex Ltd. (3C) was born from village gatherings in Kakumiro and Hoima (2010–2017), where families voiced urgent needs for clean water, clinics, education, and livelihoods. Local leaders and diaspora volunteers pooled skills in agronomy, teaching, mobilization, and fundraising. By 2018, this vision crystallized into 3C with a pledge: “No community, no idea, and no dream left behind.”

Who We Are
3C is a Uganda-based, community-owned nonprofit, limited by guarantee, founded in 2018 and incorporated in 2019.

Vision: Empower vulnerable communities to thrive in harmony with their environment.

Mission: Unlock the potential of children, youth, women, refugees, and low-income families to break cycles of poverty and build resilient, thriving communities.

Values: Global Perspective, Empowerment, Sustainability, Inclusivity, Resilience, Accountability, Partnership, Integrity.

What We Do
We work through five affiliates to deliver:
 Global networking, partnerships, and sustainable returns to fuel projects.
 Climate-smart farming, policy advocacy, and waste management.
 Organ-disease prevention, peer screening, and transplant protocols.
 Education programs, adult literacy, and scholarships for girls and marginalized learners.
 WASH initiatives, psychosocial support, agro-industrial training, HIV/AIDS & emergency care.

Our Impact
 14 hectares reforested with indigenous trees.
 23 boreholes + 60 rainwater tanks serving thousands.
 80 children supported back to school.
 40,000+ smallholder farmers engaged.
 HIV/AIDS care, psychosocial support & transplant protocols.
 Eco-enterprises linking conservation with livelihoods.

At 3C, your expertise matters, your challenges are met, and delays end here.
